text:
I found The Evewell disorganised, chaotic, and worryingly unresponsive. Nurses frequently sent urgent instructions at the end of the day, making it impossible to clarify errors or ask questions. On one occasion, I was prescribed hormone patches they already knew I was allergic to. I had to use one anyway, and despite informing them of the reaction I can have, no one followed up or checked if I was ok.

During another appointment, I was physically hurt during a scan. I reported significant pain and bleeding, and raised concerns that the sonographer lacked adequate training. There was no clinical follow-up and no safeguarding response. I really felt I was bothering them when trying to follow up about it.

Despite their polished web presence, it really seemed that they are overstretched, under-coordinated, and have lost sight of patient safety. The overall culture felt rushed, dismissive, and complacent - unacceptable at this price point, and unacceptable full stop.

label.evidence_quote: Nurses frequently sent urgent instructions at the end of the day

text: I had a brilliant egg collection with Dr Joyti who has since left the clinic. Now I know why. text:
Sadly had a bad experience with this clinic. The consultant we saw was very nice and understanding but apart from that this clinic for me was such a let down! The unnecessary delays for simple things like blood tests took weeks. We had to chase the clinic for several results. We were lied to about that they hadn’t received them yet, even though they had told us they’d received the results a few weeks prior but it was a letter from the consultant they were waiting for.

The reception staff I experienced weren’t very professional. One time I had an appointment, checked in with them and I was just passed a card machine with a payment on it to pay, without a word. Several times I was there I heard unprofessional conversations that were happening between staff around patients. Dates for appointments I had were changed several times by them/appointment booked on days I said I was not available so had to chase them to correct it. Basic things that you’d expect in any customer service environment that aren’t difficult, but especially considering it’s a fertility climic makes it even worse!
For such an advertised company I was expecting more. As they messed up/had such long delays several times with such easy things as blood tests we didn’t trust them with anything more so cut our losses and have found a clinic which is so above and beyond this one. The communication in comparison has been amazing and we completely trust our new clinic. Night and day comparison!

I would advise anyone if they don’t have a good feeling from this clinic/have issues with them to do the same as something so personal, expensive and precious you should trust the clinic you’re using wholeheartedly!

I have seen other bad reviews on this clinic appear and then disappear so, I wouldn’t be surprised if this one sadly disappears too!

label.evidence_quote: We had to chase the clinic for several results

text: Clearly some people have a good experience with this clinic however we did not. The clinic required us to get some additional medical history information before they could offer treatment which I was able to do in March. Despite me then emailing Evewell multiple times asking what the next steps were and whether treatment could go ahead after sending them the requested information, they just never responded to any communication about treatment. This resulted in us being strung along with no responses for months before we eventually cut our losses and went to a different clinic in May which has been fantastic. The only communication Evewell responded to was emails where I requested copies of the tests I did with them so that I could use them elsewhere. It is frustrating because we of course paid for me and my partners tests and the consultation which are expensive, and we wouldn’t have done this at Evewell if we knew we would be going elsewhere for treatment due to their lack of communication.

We had an unsuccessful round of IVF at the Evewell in July 2022.
IVF clinics are really in the business of selling hope. When people are spending upwards of £10k on a round of IVF there should be far more rigour and independent analysis of the 'success results' that a clinic claims.
We sort treatment due to having a low AMH. Our NHS  consultant gave us a 3% chance of success.
My advice after having 2 unsuccessful rounds (1st with the ARGC) and 2 miscarriages is to try and relax! Keep yourself healthy and try to be positive. We went on holiday and shortly after coming home we got naturally pregnant and now have a beautiful 9 week old baby!

text:
I chose to have my fertility treatment with ARGC because their published statistics looked incredible. Unfortunately, my experience did not reflect the same standard.

The clinic can be quite difficult to navigate at first, though over time you do find your feet. My first visit was for immune testing — I had even called twice beforehand to confirm the details. However, when I arrived, I was mistakenly treated as if I was there for a monitoring cycle. After some confusion and back-and-forth, I was redirected to another clinic for the correct tests.

When my results came back showing elevated NK cells, I was understandably upset. Dr. Nawal was kind, clear, and supportive, explaining the next steps thoroughly. I later decided to proceed with a TSI (timed intercourse) round with Letrozole.

While the doctors I encountered were excellent — knowledgeable, empathetic, and concise — the communication with the nursing and administrative teams was consistently poor. Messages and emails often went unanswered, sometimes for days, which during treatment can feel like an eternity. I frequently had to repeat information that was already in my file and chase updates that should have been proactively shared.

During my cycle, there were repeated issues with clarity about next steps, medication requirements, and blood testing. On one occasion, I was contacted after leaving the clinic to return and give more blood due to a sample issue. Later, I discovered I had been given insufficient progesterone pessaries for my two-week wait, despite the team knowing I would be abroad. When I reached out for help, the responses were dismissive and lacked accountability.

I eventually submitted an official complaint after returning from treatment abroad. The response I received was defensive, inaccurate in parts, and did not acknowledge the stress caused by their poor communication and administration.

When my treatment ultimately did not work, I still reached out with openness to continue. Sadly, my final interaction — a phone call with the head nurse — was met with hostility and no compassion, despite just having shared the news that my treatment had failed.

The doctors at ARGC are undeniably skilled, and I have full respect for their medical expertise. However, the administrative, nursing, and patient care teams seriously undermine the clinic’s reputation. The lack of communication, empathy, and accountability made what was already an emotionally and financially challenging process far more stressful than it needed to be.

If you are considering ARGC, I would advise going in with caution — their doctors are excellent, but their support systems for patients fall significantly short of the standard you would expect from a leading fertility clinic.

label.evidence_quote: I had even called twice beforehand

text:
ARGC Malpractice Risked My Sister’s Life:

Unfortunately, my sister received treatment at
ARGC for an in vitro fertilization procedure. Despite the protocols indicating that implantation should be avoided if there are signs of ovarian hyper-stimulation, the clinic advised her to proceed with the implantation of two embryos. As a result, she developed ascites, renal failure, and severe electrolyte imbalances, putting her life at risk.

From the onset of the first symptoms, including difficulty breathing, abdominal swelling, nausea, and intolerance to food, my sister visited the clinic multiple times. However, no additional tests were performed, and her condition was downplayed. She was simply advised to increase fluid intake.

Fortunately, my sister went to A&E, where she was attended by the critical care team. They confirmed that her condition was life-threatening. The hospital later confirmed that she was the only patient they had treated with ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome (OHSS) who had embryos implanted. As no reputable clinic would recommend implantation when the ovaries are hyperstimulated.

Currently, she is hospitalized in critical condition due to complications arising from malpractice at ARGC, which failed to apply proper preventive measures and dismissed her initial symptoms, allowing her condition to worsen and putting her life in danger.

Please carefully consider your health and well-being before choosing ARGC for your fertility treatment.

label.evidence_quote: ARGC Malpractice Risked My Sister’s Life

text:
I'm really very surprised by any of the positive reviews I've read on this site. Our experience at ARGC was so awful. Someone wrote here that it feels like they are selling hope. That's exactly how we found it. The place is hugely overcrowded and so so shabby. We were ushered in from one test to the next with little or no explantation of what it was all for. Each time we went through the door we paid more money and we didn't know why.  After the first consultation we didn't have any connection with the doctor apart from one not very pleasant scan where I asked him what my results meant. He didn't look at me and said that I had a very low chance of getting pregnant and that was it. I was devastated and left the room.

After having spent over £1000 on bloodtests over several weeks we went in to start the treatment. On this day we found out that they needed another £5000 for medication on top of the £5000 we knew about. We hadn't been told about this and when we complained they said we should have asked about how much it was going to cost - which we had done repeatedly. The price list we had been given was very obtuse and hadn't made this remotely clear. I was devastated. We didn't have this money set aside. It felt like our chance of having a baby was taken away from us and we'd thrown away the money we'd spent so far.

Some of the staff were so uncaring. You felt like you were wasting their time with a question. I felt like they just wanted our money. There was no human connection. Yes they get good results for people who are able to pay for it. But that can't be all there is. You should feel cared for and comforted and kept in the light when going through something as potentially as stressful as IVF.

But the good news is we found a clinic in the Czech Republic - in Prague called UNICA. The clinic was clean and calm with a wonderful zen like atmosphere. The doctors available to talk and tell you about your treatment at every step. The and receptionists who know your name and ask how you are. Above all you have a dedicated co-ordinator who answers any questions you have, looks after you, walks in with you before the operation and there for you at the end. She tells you what drugs you are taking and why. She gives you a little pep talk when you need it and reminds you to be realistic when you need that too. In short it was the best care we could have hoped for. For this exceptional treatment, scans and all medication, flights, accommodation, food, concerts in the most beautiful of cities and a holiday of a lifetime the whole thing came to £5000 - about £7000 less than we would have paid at ARGC. We don't yet know whether we are pregnant but the experience was magical and I'd urge anyone thinking about doing IVF to explore options abroad.

label.evidence_quote: Each time we went through the door

text:
ARGC is one of the worst clinics in the U.K. I froze my eggs with them in 2016, got 19 in total, I thought I had preserved my fertility. However in 2020, when I wanted to use my frozen eggs, Dr Sapna continued to insist we should do a fresh cycle, which made no sense to given we I had eggs saved from when I was much younger. Normally When you thaw eggs, survival rate is 90%- ARGC lost 17/19eggs while thawing them.

I have take two opinions since, one from a clinic in the US and one at St Mary’s hospital, both confirmed that their lab must be sub standard and embryologist are not trained properly given our outcome.

We requested a full investigation, which came back with “we followed protocol on the day”. After such a disastrous outcome- Dr Taranassi never spoke with us or emailed us ( even though we requested it). Please do yourself a favour and pick a clinic with modern lab like Care Fertility/CRGH or anyone else really. Absolutely AVOID ARGC- substandard is the only description.

label.evidence_quote: lost 17/19 eggs while thawing

text:
Trying to build a family through fertility treatment is already exhausting and heartbreaking. What we didn’t expect was to also have to manage a clinic.

Our experience at Create Fertility (St Paul’s, London) has been marked by poor communication and a lack of coordination. The clinic’s “patient coordinator” model hasn’t functioned as a support system for us—there have been long stretches with no proactive contact, no clear ownership of next steps, and uncertainty about who is responsible for our case. Instead of feeling guided, we’ve often felt we were chasing information and stitching the process together ourselves.

Appointments have also felt poorly managed, with delays that add even more strain to days that are already emotionally loaded. When you need answers quickly, getting hold of the clinic can be difficult, and responses are not consistently timely.

If you are pursuing the egg donor route that involves working with IVI Madrid, be aware that coordination between clinics may fall heavily on you as the patient. The contrast has been noticeable: the Madrid-side transfer process has been efficient, and the blood draw staff in London have been consistently good. Unfortunately, the overall London-side support has not matched what this kind of journey demands.

This review is to help future patients know what to expect and to encourage Create to improve the basic standards of communication, continuity, and care.

UPDATE: I emailed a complaint in about the lack of care I'm receiving with hopes that it would help progress our treatment. Instead, I'm told that the the complaint needs to be resolved BEFORE my treatment can resume!! So my treatment hinges on Create fixing poor internal processes that have been on going for years now.  I feel that I am being penalized as a patient for raising a concern.  Despite raising a complaint, I have yet to hear from anyone for days now.  Time is ticking away and we're not closer to having the family we dreamed of.

label.evidence_quote: no clear ownership of next steps

Ended up failed with my non-refundable cycle, they blamed me for being too old and could unlikely never have my own biological child ever. I felt publicly humiliated when the embryologist told me all of these, and so I had formally complained this clinic to HFEA. Then, I had a mental breakdown and suffered 1 month of hospitalisation and further 6 months of recovery. During this time, I had my current boyfriend who encouraged me to try conceive naturally with him. I got pregnant after first try, now I have our lovely daughter in my arms. This clinic is a highly commercialised and they can falsely make you feel good about yourself or have wrong perception about your fertility, so you put your money down to their services. However, their embryologists were incredibly unprofessional and nasty when things aren't going smoothly. BE VERY CAREFUL with choosing your clinic and services if you want to go through full IVFs.

text:
⭐️ 1 Star

I would strongly urge anyone considering CREATE Fertility to think very carefully before proceeding.

Our experience has been characterised by poor communication, a complete lack of coordination between departments, and a level of patient care that has steadily deteriorated over time.

We were led to believe we would be proceeding with a frozen embryo transfer this cycle, took time off work for appointments, and only at the last minute were told that basic prerequisite steps had not even been arranged. These were things that should have been identified and coordinated well in advance. The result was an entirely avoidable delay to treatment.

What has been most shocking is the complete inability to get hold of our assigned patient coordinator. Despite repeated emails, phone calls, formal complaints, and requests for escalation, weeks have passed with virtually no meaningful communication. Multiple staff members acknowledged internally that responses were overdue and that management had been “chased”, yet still nobody contacted us.

The repeated explanation given to us has been that the clinic is “exceptionally busy”. Frankly, if that is the case then the clinic has clearly taken on more patients than it can adequately support and manage appropriately. As private patients undergoing fertility treatment, we do not believe it is unreasonable to expect basic communication, coordination, and continuity of care.

Every department appears to operate in isolation, with responsibility constantly shifting elsewhere while no one takes ownership. The role of a patient coordinator should surely be to bridge those gaps, yet in our experience that coordination has been entirely absent.

Sadly, this sits within wider concerns we have had regarding clinical decision-making and informed consent. We were advised to freeze one embryo at day 3, while the remaining embryos continued in culture but ultimately did not progress to blastocyst stage. This frozen embryo is now our one and only embryo.

Looking back, we do not feel we were provided with sufficiently balanced or patient-specific information to make a truly informed decision regarding freezing at day 3 versus continuing culture to day 5. We were later advised there is no clear evidence base demonstrating improved outcomes with this approach, yet the implications of the decision — emotionally, financially, and practically — were never properly explained to us.

Most concerningly, during a follow-up appointment after egg collection, the consultant openly acknowledged that the clinic receives “lots of complaints” regarding this practice and stated they “might be changing” it. Hearing that after the fact only reinforced our feeling that concerns around this approach were already well known internally, yet we were not properly informed beforehand.

As non-medical patients, you place enormous trust in a fertility clinic to guide you through hugely emotional and complex decisions. Instead, we have been left dealing with months of uncertainty surrounding the viability of our only embryo, while continuing to pay storage fees and repeatedly chase for basic communication.

At times, the clinic has felt less like a healthcare provider focused on patient care and more like a profit-driven operation, where communication and support rapidly decline once patients are within the system. The repeated delays, lack of accountability, ongoing storage fees, and constant need for us to chase updates have only reinforced that feeling.

At this stage, we feel completely powerless. CREATE currently has our one and only embryo in storage, while we struggle simply to get responses or move treatment forward. We are now actively exploring transferring our embryo to another clinic because we no longer have confidence in the level of care, communication, or organisation being provided.

Fertility treatment is emotionally exhausting enough without feeling ignored, unsupported, and passed between departments. We would not recommend this clinic.

label.evidence_quote: complete lack of coordination between departments

Initially if you have relatively few embryos it is tempting to bypass it, but this is a false saving as it can cost you in so many other ways. They are the ones who should know that, but never had any advice from them on that point. Doctors and facilities fine though patient coordination variable. Prices (relatively speaking) reasonable. But you are on a conveyor belt and get standard treatment, no attention to male vitamins and you could well end up wondering whether there are investigations they haven't tried.

text:
I'd like to express that I feel disappointed finding out about the procedure Create takes when deciding/offering to freeze an embryo on day 3. When we received the call to ask as if we would like to freeze that embryo, we were told that it was a good quality. Later one, we found out that this embryo would still have to go to a previous and additional process of thawing before being transfer in which there is a possibility it could be lost, i.e. it cannot have a good quality to be transfer. During one of the last calls, I had with a nurse, I was told that this is normal procedure of Create. To decide to freeze embryos on day 3. I was also told that other clinics do not do this anymore, while Create still does. I felt disappointed when given this information as this can be easily interpreted as being given higher hopes. This procedure, I was told, is done to prevent or to make sure there is one embryo frozen. However, this is not 100% accurate as at that moment, we as a couple, or other couples, do not know that there is an additional step required for the embryo to be transferred. In other words, I feel like I have two embryos frozen (chances for a potential pregnancy) and one maybe. As you can imagine, this feels disheartening considering the high financial investment and effort put into this procedure.  Furthermore, the day 3 embryo, after three days of cultivation, it did not survive and was discarded. The quality was not good enough to freeze again. We keep asking ourselves why wasn't it cultivated from the beginning further? We have ask Create and we still get no answer. We've been told that this was in perfect quality to freeze and it was done just in case none of the other would survive. This creates uncertainty. I interpret is as a way to say add an extra 'successful' frozen embryo as a result of the treatment when in reality it isn't! Therefore, I highly suggest to couples to let their embryos develop until day 5 or until they are in great quality for direct transfer.

Regarding the communication, I agree with other comments here. The communication in English of some foreign professionals is poor. I am a foreigner as well, working in a foreign country; however, I make the effort or have had courses to help me achieve a better performance. The fixed line has had technical issues, but the main problem was their language skills. If these people are communicating such sensitive information to couples, some actions could be taken by Create (i.e. training) to improve.

Regarding the service in St Paul's, all has been well. Just some slight delays in early morning appointments 5-10 min late.
The team does stay in touch, calls/emails to follow-up on the procedure, and the staff is very friendly/amicable.

My chose of stars is related to the disappointment of the guidance for freezing embryos. It would be better to be more transparent and open for this matter with the couples.

Update 14.12.21:
After this review, we received a call from the lead consultant. We must say that our comments were always taking into consideration and we did receive some additional feedback before. However, in some cases, the extra info provided, did not make things better. After this new and last call  on Nov. 22, my husband and I felt satisfied with the explanations that were given.

Nevertheless, my comments and rating above remain. Reviews are meant to help the company improve and to share experiences with future customers. I've have stated the good and the bad, and I hope that this helps couples to inform themselves well about this part of the process and also Create to improve their customer service.

Communication is key, and this was part of the problem, technically (with the lines) and the way is was delivered (communications skills/fluency in the language)

label.evidence_quote: disappointed finding out about the procedure

text:
Long unnecessary waiting times. Many of the workers can't speak clearly enunciated English. You can sense the disorganisation.

Invoicing seems a focus of the service rather than ensuring a smooth efficient process that respects the time of clients.

Underground laboratorial setting has no natural light and feels overly “clinical”.

Location is very good and ideally placed in central London..

Out of principle, it's worth saying that this business model is exploitative and takes full advantage of desperate people looking to conceive. It's a money extraction racket backed by government statutes that will set people back thousands for an outcome that isn't even guaranteed.

By them making home DIY artificial insemination less legally protected, the gov is funnelling people into this disgusting cash cow industry and taking their cut through taxes.

Also IVF has a much higher rate of a child emerging with significant issues. This is because IVF selects imperfect “lazy” sperm to fertilize an egg. Nature, as usual, does things better.

text:
I received treatment with Create St Paul’s in June of 2025 and since moving clinics wanted to leave a review of my experience. I’ll start by saying that all of the healthcare professionals I encountered there were very kind, considerate and extremely supportive in helping me through my first experience of IVF.

After a lot of research we decided to go with Create. A huge deciding factor in this was its location with it having a clinic in St Albans as well as central London. I have a fairly hectic work schedule so this was important to minimise stress around apts. Unfortunately throughout the process we only had two apt. in St Albans one of which was cancelled so I would not take this location as much to do with the clinic.

The difficulty we had with create was around the clinic being very unorganised and lacking in personalised care. You see a different Dr at every appointment leading to a lack of consistency. The biggest issue for us was the patient coordinators. The idea is you are assigned someone who supports you throughout the process who helps with all of the admin side.

Our patient coordinator changed 4 times and were completely unorganised to the point where I often had to follow up and chase up admin and apts. This added a lot more stress into the process, managing everything alongside work and I felt I couldn’t trust them to be on top of everything.

Following our unsuccessful results the doctors did a review and said this was a case of ‘luck’ and they recommended we go again with the same protocol. Knowing what I know now, I am absolutely baffled with this response as there were a whole host of easy diagnostic tests we could have done and things we could have tweaked moving forward. We have since found a different clinic where we had drastically different positive results.

Looking back at the experience with create, I feel they are a very run of the mill IVF clinic that won’t be able to go above a very basic IVF protocol. They offer treatment for NHS and private patients however, I feel we very much got an NHS experience but at the price of a private clinic.

label.evidence_quote: all of the healthcare professionals were very kind

text:
Clinic lacks transparency, accountability and management. They failed to make me aware that they freeze FOUR eggs per straw and that you will have you pay for additional straws used. They convinently told me this AFTER paying for an initial consultation of £210. I have had consultation with 3 clinics and have asked embryologists if they ever freeze fours eggs in a single straw, and the answer has been a resounding NO - 1 is the practice and 2 is the absolute maximum per straw. I tried raising the fact that I had been uninformed of these things and wouldn't have gone with their clinic if I was informed of their practice of freezing four eggs per straw, but it was brushed aside.

I have also experienced a repeated lack of follow-up with the clinic on a few things. To date (entering September), I have not received the letter from my consultation I had back in July despite asking for it twice. I have always had clinics send this out promptly following my consultations, a day after and at the most 5 days.

Reply:

Following my consultation on 18 July 2024, I reached out on 26 July 2024 on the app informing your staffs that I am waiting to be contacted for costing before I proceeded, where I also asked for the summary of my consultation and for Dr Fatum to answer a quick question relating to my treatment plan. Come 2 August 2024, I still do not have an answer to my question nor the summary of my consultation to this very day (nearing end Sep). I explicitly asked if the clinic could first let me know the answer to my question so that I'll be better informed about how many cycles I'll be proceeding with (and then go over the costs). Once again, I do not hear back and I reach out 6 August 2024, asking for an update.

Your clinic had decided not to publish anywhere in your pricing list or communicated at any point BEFORE my consultation that a maximum of four straws are used in your treatment and that any further straws are chargeable (and that it freezes FOUR eggs per straw). This was communicated to me AFTER my initial consultation, and in vain, I had to get my husband to take a day off from work to drive down from Leeds to London.

I explained on 12 August 2024 on the app, that I was uninformed of these and would not have considered your clinic if I had been aware that your clinic freezes multiple eggs per straw and that it was charging for any more than four straws used to freeze a maximum of four eggs per straw.

This is not something I can accept. I wrote you 5 September 2024 that given that these pieces of information was withheld from me before I had to spend on a consultation in vain, I asked for a refund of the cost of my consultation and reimbursement for my travel cost to attend that appointment. I expressed that I am no longer interested in receiving the summary of my consultation (which I have yet to receive to date, close to three months later) nor pursuing treatment with your clinic for lapses in transparency, accountability and management. I expressed that I wished to move on from this disappointing experience with your clinic. Once again, your clinic hasn't cared to respond to my email. Given how your clinic has repeatedly and blatantly ignored my emails, I will be escalating this to HEFA and providing my honest feedback of my experiences with your clinic.

label.evidence_quote: failed to make me aware that they freeze FOUR eggs per straw

text:
The actual clinic and the staff were amazing, I have nothing but praise for them and would happily give a 5 star. It is a shame i cannot separate my review for the consultant and the clinic.  We had one round of IVF and for a forward thinking clinic that uses AI  and an app, they need to ensure their consultants use the app for all medication updates and appointment updates (as IVF veterans will know there are many medications at different doses). There is no point advertising that there is an app, or live feeds for embryo development if they are not used or available  for EVERY patient (transparency is very important) . They also need to ensure they partner with consultants that have time to dedicate to IVF patients as everything is time critical and this is very important, as at a regular IVF clinic the staff are available for contact as soon as a patient is at day 1 of their period. It is extremely important to be allowed a safe space to be allowed to ask questions, we did not feel we were given this by the consultant we used. She did not react well to questions, and the one time I did get emotional (mainly because of how I was being treated) she told me I needed to get help for mental health issues.
The receptionist used by the consultant is not contactable by phone, only responds via email, as does the consultant. If you are the kind if patient that needs to be able to contact someone by phone then AVOID.
We appreciate the tests requested by our consultant and the recommendation for a hysteroscopy. We often had to chase up results and were told they had already been sent (we had no record and nothing in our junk mail). We continued our treatment with the same consultant as we had already paid over £2000 for the medications (lot of which were unnecessary add-ons).
We appreciate all of the help and advice we received from the clinic and once they were involved things improved dramatically, the rooms were really nice and it has the potential to be an amazing clinic if working with right consultants.
Also high praise for the embryologists at Avenues they tried everything they could to ensure we had an embryo to transfer (a day 3 6 cell) their communication was excellent.
We have since moved to a new clinic/consultant and have had much better results so far (day 5 blastocyst) and less a aggressive treatment protocol.

label.evidence_quote: consultants use the app for all medication updates

text:
I had a very disappointing experience at The Birth Company under HCA Healthcare UK today (27Feb2025), specifically due to the unprofessional and rude behavior of Specialist Sonographer Afsaneh Tehrani. This was my first pregnancy, a deeply emotional and special moment, yet she treated me in an unwelcoming and dismissive manner.

When I asked if the sound we heard was my baby’s heartbeat, she responded in a condescending way: “Your heart is up there” (pointing at my chest), “that’s the baby’s heartbeat, yeah!” This was completely unnecessary and unprofessional. A first-time mother asking a simple question should never be made to feel belittled. On top of that, she didn’t even acknowledge my pregnancy with a simple “congratulations,” which is basic courtesy in any prenatal setting.

She was also clearly in a hurry, rushing through the appointment as if she just wanted to get it over with. At one point, she even asked if I had finished changing yet, making me feel pressured and uncomfortable instead of supported during such an important moment.

Additionally, she spoke very aggressively to my husband, telling him to only take pictures without her in the frame. While we understand staff may have preferences about photos, the way she communicated this was unnecessarily harsh and unpleasant, adding to the overall negative experience.

To be clear, the reception and administrative staff were kind and professional, and my issue is solely with Afsaneh Tehrani. I am well aware of the exceptional quality of care in HCA Healthcare, as I will be delivering at The Portland Hospital. Unfortunately, this experience fell far below those standards.

I strongly do not recommend Afsaneh Tehrani and hope The Birth Company ensures their patients receive the respect and compassion they deserve during such a sensitive and emotional time.

label.evidence_quote: unprofessional and rude behavior

text:
Whilst this clinic may be good at dealing with other / straightforward gynaecological matters, I found the consultant I saw to be disappointing. She was truly compassionate in terms of the physical pain and emotional distress I was in which was great, but was lacking in medical knowledge around things I would expect any gynaecologist to be well versed in. She was unable to diagnose a textbook case of vulvodynia, looked up on her computer how to deal with recurrent thrush, and when given a full history of my symptoms and concerns, said multiple times that it's a 'mystery' - not comforting words to hear from a specialist you've paid substantial money to see. Ultimately, after the tests, the mystery remained, the explanation was insufficient, and the treatment suggested was not suitable and did not work. She did not get to the bottom of the issue. I eventually got an answer and solution from the NHS a few months later from a gynaecologist that knew her stuff.

Regarding the clinic itself, the only nice thing I can say is that the waiting room was nice and well equipped. Aside from this, waiting times were terrible - especially given this is a private clinic. Communication about delays was poor and on one occasion, I travelled in, waited a long time, only for the consultant to then tell me that the test results I'd come in to discuss had not even been received and we had nothing to discuss. This was known prior to my appointment and should have been communicated. They then said they would not charge for that appointment, but still did.

Overall, I would not recommend this clinic. Other consultants here might be good but even the waiting times and terrible communication is enough to put me off. There are plenty of better private clinics to go in London.

text:
I initially contacted The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy due to a misunderstanding with the contact information I found online. Despite this mix-up, the admin staff was eager to assist, strongly recommending Dr. Amin Gorgy as an exceptional option. Encouraged by their enthusiasm, and after discussing it with my husband, we decided to book an appointment for clinical advice regarding our fertility concerns.

Our appointment began later than planned, highlighting an initial disregard for patient time. Punctuality is crucial to us, and this delay was not a positive start.

During the consultation with Dr. Gorgy, it became evident that there was a lack of empathy towards our situation. The doctor discussed Intrauterine Insemination, emphasising its low success rate, and quoted the treatment at £1,500 to £2,000, which is considerably higher than the £1,100 listed on their website. Similarly, for IVF treatment, he quoted £6,000, significantly above the website's prices of £3,800 for IVF and £4,750 for an IVF-One Cycle Package. This pattern of quoting higher prices than those listed led us to suspect an intentional strategy to increase patient expenditure, undermining our trust in the clinic's transparency and honesty.

Furthermore, when my husband requested a written report of our assessment, Dr. Gorgy informed us that the clinic only provides verbal feedback, denying us any tangible documentation of our consultation. This approach felt dismissive and left us without essential reference material.

My husband was advised to get hormone tests, and the finance team advised us to use their in-house lab for these tests. Before finalizing any decisions, we carefully reviewed the costs involved. In the end, we were confronted with a billing summary of £760, comprising £280 for the consultation and an unexpectedly high £480 for the blood tests. This was in stark contrast to the anticipated costs, especially since the basic hormone blood tests were listed at £70 on their website. Yet, we were quoted £240 under the "Hormone Profile" category, with an additional two tests at £120 each. Given these discrepancies and the lack of clear justification for the extra charges, we decided not to proceed with the blood tests, sensing something fishy in their billing practices. This decision was driven by the dramatic difference in expected versus actual billing, which raised significant concerns about the clinic's transparency and integrity. It appeared as though there was a deliberate attempt to manipulate billing categories for financial gain, indicating a profoundly troubling approach to financial practices.

Our interaction with the admin staff and the accounting team, especially concerning the lab tests, further compounded our decision not to pursue treatment at this clinic. The perceived enthusiasm of the admin staff felt insincere upon leaving, and the pressuring tactics of the accounting team regarding the lab tests were particularly off-putting.

In conclusion, our visit to The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy was disappointing. We encountered a lack of empathy, flexibility, and a respect for our autonomy and time. The inconsistent pricing and the refusal to provide written assessments, coupled with a concerning billing experience, diminished our trust in the clinic. We felt viewed not as individuals in need of support but as potential sources of increased revenue.

Based on our experience, we cannot recommend The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy. We will seek assistance elsewhere, where our health and well-being are prioritised, and transparency is a given.

label.evidence_quote: appointment began later than planned

text: Me and my partner had a video consultation with the doctor. (She had several treatments done at the clinic before, so the doctor  knows her medical history.) Our agenda of the consultation was to discuss results of tests that were recommended by the doctor. Not to mention that each consolation with the doctor (each time you need to address any question to the doctor) is extremely expensive 170+ pounds. When such a high premium is paid you expect to get a corresponding service. 50% of scheduled 30 min zoom talk both audio and video just froze - we could not hear anything. During the rest of the time we were able to formulate a few questions. Almost none of the questions were answered. Definitely nothing specific, no reasoning at all. When a person claims to be a specialist in a certain field,  immunology in this case, he/she should be able to interpret both clinical history of a patient and results of tests and and at least try to connects the dots - to provide some sort of reasoning, which makes him believe that a certain medication is of a benefit. I didn't get a feeling that a person I was talking to had good knowledge  of immunology. It was rather: try this medication because (we can provide it) and in general it works better than other medications - very business oriented. One of test was LAD - and after some hours of researching and reading replies (that are publicity available) from other doctors to patients with similar test results and clinical history, I'm almost 100% that the doctor didn't even highlight result that were of relevance, but instead pointed to a completely different direction. Luckily for people working in this field there are not so many medications to chose from , and therefore even people with a limited knowledge of the domain can "successfully" operate in this field.

label.evidence_quote: 50% of scheduled 3

After i came to terms with the lack of the actual result ; My later regret was combined with both anger for the waste of money but mostly for being given a false hope by what can only be described  a totally unsympathetic and robotic doctor (along with his mismanagement and misadvise on my situation). 3 unsuccessful IVF cycles and money down the drain (where I was charged for the 3rd cycle  that involved nothing but one scan and one "supposed egg extraction" ) I later learnt that they could have just advised against the last IVF cycle completely in order not to waste my money and not to give me the false hope..... We couldn't help it but my husband and I both felt somewhat the "extortion approach" but alas also knew that we were partially to blame for  trusting ourselves into the incompetence of these doctors. My advise to other women out there, do your research promptly and thoroughly before committing to your IFV team....I will add though that some of the less senior members of staff at the clinic were pleasant and understanding....so justly.. i wont say much negative about them...

text:
I have had two treatments of IUI with Dr. Gorgy after someone I knew had a number of treatments with him. Unfortunately, we don't exactly share the same experience and I can relate to the other reviews that the whole experience is very money driven.

Dr. Gorgy takes very little into account your past history and hardly writes down any notes or remembers anything from previous appointments. This is something i was prepped for by my friend who attended the clinic, so I knew to write a diary for each appointment.

I had one cycle of IUI with Gorgy which all went well and all to plan, however no pregnancy at no fault of the clinic. Sadly the second cycle did not go as well, where he completely missed my ovulation by a couple days. Somehow I was convinced to proceed with my IUI, and out of desperation I agreed. I felt mislead as the chances are actually known to be very small when performing IUI a couple days after ovulation which was not mentioned on the day.

During this cycle I ended up hyperstimulated and suspected for an ectopic pregnancy for an event that happened prior to this IUI. I notified the nurses that I was going away around this period and no advice was given not to travel when I was due to fly 7 hours overseas. Had my pregnancy turned out to be ectopic I might have died mid flight.

Unfortunately, while abroad I ended up in hospital due illness from being hyperstimuled and also underwent investigations for an ectopic pregenancy which turned out to be negative. From the experience I found it really amazing how the medical clinic treating me did not advise me not to travel when I mentioned it to them, particularly when it comes to potentially life threatening issues.

Another review mentions the Dr. being late, I can vouch for this as on three occasions I arrived for an ultrasound over my two treatments with Dr. Gorgy not being there that day when promised and then and just passively sent for a blood test alone.

Personally, I found the majority of the staff apart from Jeana a bit ignorant. On a number of occasions I had texted the nurses during my miscarriage and they did not reply back to my texts.

Lastly, a particularly uncomfortable situation being sent into a basement for payment each session where the staff don't have the courtesy to even look you in the eye. I felt like that handing over a couple thousand was a nuisance to them.

Since I've had treatment with the NHS and I can hands down say that the treatment is ten folds better. Sadly, I won't be recommending this clinic as I felt there was serious lack of care.

label.evidence_quote: Dr. Gorgy takes very little into account your past history

text:
I have waited a while to write this review for various reasons. However, given the potential negative impact on many other women who might have the same misfortune of coming across Dr Sarah Glynne, I thought I must follow up with a review.

I went to see Sarah Glynne following some hormonal issues post covid. During the appointment, I was told that I was in "early perimenopause", despite having impeccable blood work and no other evidence outside of a range of symptoms (some of which overlap with perimenopause, but can also be seen in Long covid). She then suggested I would need to be on HRT until age 51 (I am 30). She told me that she would keep upping my dose up to a maximum, until I find relief from my symptoms. I was also told that lifestyle adjustments cannot balance hormones and have no impact. She told me that covid damages the ovaries and that they only "bounce back in younger women".

Naturally, I left the appointment very demoralised to say the least.

I then consulted a highly regarded and, unlike Sarah Glynne, actually qualified Gynaecologist, to get a second opinion. Long story short and to paraphrase, he said that her recommendations and assessment were NONSENSE. There is no evidence that I am in perimenopause and also no evidence of Long covid having lasting impact on ovaries. He also told me that my symptoms would be different in frequency and cadence if it was indeed perimenopause. And that my symptoms were more likely a result of dysregulation post covid, which can also impact hormonal balance (but this is NOT perimenopause). He prescribed me a different treatment option which was much more sensible, and also recommended lifestyle changes as he believes these DO make a big difference. And indeed they have done. Sarah Glynne was entirely wrong in her assessment and her recommendations were dangerous.

I am writing this because:

- A. I want my money back for the £300 consultation which was not only a waste of money but also caused more harm than good (especially to my mental health). I am also grateful I followed my gut and did NOT go on HRT given the risks.

- B. I do not want other poor young women to come across Sarah Glynne and have the misfortune to be told they are perimenopausal when this is NOT the case.

To conclude, I believe women struggling with post covid issues with hormones consult a qualified Gynaecologist before making such conclusions about their health, and taking actions that can cause serious harm.

Sarah Glynne has clearly ignored the Hippocratic oath of "first do no harm", as harm she did cause. I do hope Claire Mellon & Associates offer reasonable compensation following this message, as I do not want to escalate things further.

label.evidence_quote: I was told that I was in early perimenopause

I’m extremely disappointed with the level of customer service I’ve received from 25 Harley Street Gynaecology Clinic. When you’re paying Harley Street prices, you expect a standard of service that reflects that – unfortunately, that’s far from what I experienced.

While Dr. Khan was lovely and professional, the same cannot be said for the administrative side of the clinic. One of my blood tests had to be re-done due to what appears to be misplacement or contamination, and although two of the tests were processed, one wasn’t – leading to an unnecessary return trip.

Even worse, I had a follow-up appointment scheduled, and I received no call from the doctor. I emailed – no reply. I called – I was told my results weren’t ready and I’d simply have to wait longer. At this point, it has been more than 10 days, and I have no clarity, no results, and no follow-up. All this after spending £900 on a consultation and tests.

This is not what you expect from a Harley Street clinic – communication has been terrible, and the lack of responsiveness is completely unacceptable. I’m genuinely upset with how I’ve been treated and would caution others to carefully consider whether this clinic can truly offer the level of care and professionalism their pricing suggests.

text:
After reading some great reviews I’m sorry to say that I had a rather poor experience with Dr Khan and Harley Street Gynaecology.

I felt that in my first consultation he was ok, quite blunt, but ok. It became quite clear that in the second consultation he hadn’t all of read my test results and was dropping in quite big diagnosis as he was reading my results whilst on the call. I had to ask “what about my blood tests?” It was all very rushed and disorganised. I also really didn’t appreciate the tone in which he was speaking to me, which is not at all that of a caring doctor dealing with sensitive issues. Having spoken to a number of my female friends, they have been taken aback by some of the things I was told.

There were a number of mix ups with my appointment times and I had 2 calls outside of agreed times. My appointment was brought forward because of an emergency (which I would have thought an emergency would result in pushing an appointment back?) that’s fine as these things happen, but this wasn’t communicated to me at all so i missed one of my calls and then became nervous when my second call was missed and i had been waiting for 45 minutes. My next call happened outside of the new time that was agreed to.

I was sent a blood test for cancer over email without this being explained to me first. That clearly caused me concern and was really the last straw.

Finally the doctor’s letter was extremely blunt and not at all what I’ve come to expect based on my experience with other clinicians. It caused me more worry than anything.

I decided to get a second opinion and I couldn’t be happier. In the space of 2 days, I’ve had my surgery, extensive tests and I have a great plan in place with a caring doctor who has put to bed some of the sweeping diagnosis that Dr Khan gave me.

label.evidence_quote: he hadn’t all of read my test results

text:
The experience I had at Harley Street Gynaecology was horrendous. I booked with Mr Khan a) because of the reviews the clinic has and b) because when booking into a clinic on Harley Street, one would expect the best care. What I can't understand is how the reviews describe Mr Khan as unhurried, friendly, and truly caring. I had the complete opposite experience.

Firstly, and most importantly, Mr Khan gave me an incorrect prescription. The dosage was unclear and it would have been extremely dangerous for me to take what was written on the prescription. When I spoke to Nicky, she and Mr Khan denied that they had given me the incorrect prescription and took no responsibility, instead simply blaming the pharmacy. Nicky asked me why I didn't just use the online pharmarcy. I was not told about the online pharmacy (because the appointment was so hurried?). In fact, I specifically asked Mr Khan where I should collect the prescription and he said my local pharmacy. Luckily, I double checked the dosage I was being prescribed online but this was not at all clear. After paying £140 for the incorrect prescription, I was grateful that Mr Khan offered me a £100 reimbursement but in the same phonecall, Nicky then asked me to leave a review, stating that Mr Khan had (I quote) "bent over backwards" to help me. This NOT the case, as it was Mr Khan's fault that I received the wrong prescription in the first place.

It says on the website that the initial consultation should last around 30 minutes. Well, I was in the waiting room for 45 minutes, but only 5-10 minutes for the consultation, in which time my file was read (surely this should have been read before). I didn't meet the friendly man that Mr Kahn is described to be. He barely looked at me, asked me a few questions to which he had no reply to, and didn't smile once. I actually felt quite uncomfortable sat in his office.

I have paid almost £1000 for 10 minutes with Mr Khan, a blood test and a cervical screening, as well as medication that has not worked for me - it has in fact done the absolute opposite of what I was hoping for. I understand that there isn't a clear fix and you have to try different things sometimes, but it feels like there is absolutely no care and it is instead all about the money. Surely there should be some check in or follow up to see how I'm doing/reacting to the medication - especially for the price paid. What Mr Khan prescribed me has had a horrible effect on me and I'm in a really bad way. You have to pay a further £200 for a follow up consultation, otherwise you'll never have any check-in to see how it's all going. I'm very happy to pay this for a clinic that is actually good, but not this one. I emailed Nicky to see whether I was likely going to have to have more tests in a follow up consultation (meaning more money, so I was just checking) and was instead phoned to book in, as Nicky said she's "not medical" so I was asked to pay £200 upfront and book.

Nicky comes across as a very nice person throughout the process, until you say anything slightly negative about your experience/Mr Khan, at which point she turns very unprofessional and blames your bad experience on you. To quote from one of her emails to me when I emailed to complain (please note that I did not ask for any money back or "blackmail" them as Nicky accused me of. "What is it you want from him? He already has given you money off which I told him for…. Sorry I can not abide someone speaking so awfully about him when it is not true. So as for the previous email please go ahead Miss Jones, no thank you and free to do what ever you want but blackmail – Absolutely NOT. So disgusting and shocked Miss Jones."

The only thing I got out of the appointment was finding out I have a condition that the cervical screening picked up but I was told nothing about the condition or advised how to deal with it at all. There are much better clinics out there so do not waste your time or energy on this one.

EDIT: I didn't leave positive feedback prior to this and was not offered a reduced consultation fee. Lies.

label.evidence_quote: Mr Khan gave me an incorrect prescription

text:
I’ve been dealing with a chronic issue for a long time, and after exhausting my options through the NHS, I decided to invest in private healthcare because I wanted this problem gone. Choosing Harley Street was a major financial decision for me, especially as it is outside of my budget range, but I believed it would provide a higher standard of care and service that would help me.
Unfortunately, the experience has been far below my expectations.
The clinic initially provided the pharmacist with the wrong prescription details. During this time, I contacted the clinic several times with basic questions because I was confused about my prescriptions, yet responses were slow and sometimes took days. Eventually, the pharmacist informed me that the clinic had sent the wrong prescription information.
I was then told that a corrected prescription would be issued. However, it has now been over a week of me chasing the clinic with no resolution, no meaningful updates, and very poor communication throughout. The only emails I have consistently received were requests to pay for the prescription again in order for it to be sent to me, despite the issue originating from the clinic’s mistake. Realistically, this should have been resolved immediately and the corrected prescription sent without expecting me to pay again.
What makes this even more frustrating is that before payment was made, I received repeated duplicate emails chasing invoices for both the service and medication. Yet when I needed support, advice, or clarification, responses became slow, blunt, and lacking empathy.
The overall tone of the service has felt cold and impersonal. I am not from a wealthy background — I came here hoping this treatment would genuinely improve my quality of life, and I have spent a significant amount of money, I’ve even travelled from one city to another to make that happen. Because of that, this experience has been incredibly disappointing.

text:
We attended The Lister in November 2023 for IVF embryo treatment.

The Lister excels in providing a conveyor belt approach that often leaves you waiting 1-2 hours for each appointment. The final week leading up to egg collection I was required to come in for blood tests and ultrasound monitoring every day, fair enough. But each time meant 2-3, sometimes even 4 hours of my day gone.

The in-patient experience and process after extraction was poorly monitored, with an extremely painful recovery. They gave me a very bungled catheter treatment to help me pass water after egg collection, and the following day I developed a high fever and infection, evidently I was going through OHSS. Honestly I’ve had far better and more competent general in-patient hospital treatments by the NHS than here.

In the end they extracted 30 eggs, of the 30 only 16 were usable for ICSI. Of those only 4 became embryos, and after genetic testing only 2 were good, one of which was just a B grade embryo. Pretty unimpressive results to go from 30 follicles stimulated and 30 eggs collected to just 2 embryos, only one of which was of an average A quality. They expressed surprise themselves and then quickly dismissed it as being down to my egg quality and or my partner’s sperm health.

I don’t believe the quality of the result was our fault. Other clinics help you prepare your body in advance and tailor the hormones far better for the best possible chance of having good egg quality retrieval. This place was just a conveyor belt and treated you mostly like everyone else.

Whilst we have 2 embryos, it was a painful and impersonal experience with very unimpressive results.

Expensive, potentially dangerous and not worth it. There are better clinics out there - avoid this one and do your research.

label.evidence_quote: waiting 1-2 hours for each appointment

text:
My experience at Lister Fertility Clinic was one of repeated disappointment, mismanagement, and emotional distress—this review only touches the surface.

Despite giving the clinic multiple chances, I was repeatedly let down.
It’s deeply upsetting to admit how vulnerable I was—trusting them not once, but three times. That’s my mistake to own, but no patient should be left feeling foolish for placing trust in a medical team.

While I do appreciate Mr. Salim’s expertise, patient care - it becomes distressingly irrelevant when the wider team fails so profoundly.

The embryology team was shockingly unempathetic—conversations felt cold and transactional, with no recognition of the emotional weight involved.

I was issued the wrong prescription, and only avoided wasting more time, risking my health, and incurring further financial harm because I caught the error myself.

One nurse outright refused to write down my protocol during our consultation and dismissed me rudely.

By the time the team finally followed up, my original sperm donor had sold out. I could have left the clinic then—but gave them the benefit of the doubt. I was later offered a replacement donor that required ICSI by the third cycle, only to discover—after the fact—that the donor company used by Lister includes a disclaimer stating they are not responsible if the sample fails to meet advertised motility. The clinic itself wasn’t even aware of this at the time and had to chase the information.
They offered a compensatory amount that was effectively cancelled out by rising costs—a complete mockery of my financial and emotional losses.

This level of risk and ambiguity was never explained to me before making such a significant decision. Only to be brushed off , that ICSI is to my advantage.. again another inconsistency , when initially I was told no avoid ICSI!

Further inconsistencies - At my first consultation, I was told IUI wasn’t a feasible option for me —only to later be advised to pursue it after three failed IVF cycles. At which point is there any consideration of the impact of the suggestion ? None. Sadly. None!

I was also charged for injections I didn’t receive, without any prior explanation or warning. After a miscarriage, I was left paying for medication I no longer needed, and was told by a nurse to “just use it for your next one”—completely ignoring what was physically or emotionally possible, and well beyond her role to advise.

It took over four weeks to receive my clinic notes, and only after my gynaecologist’s team chased them. Otherwise, I would have faced even longer delays. I was promised a call from a quality manager, only to be dismissively asked, “Didn’t you get the email?”—no follow-up, no courtesy, no accountability.

It’s clear that some patients at this private clinic are treated as financial numbers—not people. The only positive interactions I had were with the counsellor, who ended up supporting me more through the pain caused by the clinic than through the IVF itself, and one senior nurse, who was kind and empathetic—a rare exception.

If you value clarity, consistency, dignity, or even basic manners, please don’t come here.

The only thing this clinic has consistently delivered is the swift convincing and withdrawal of funds.

Proceed with caution—this is only the tip of the iceberg.

It’s deeply humiliating to share something so personal, knowing people I know may see it—but I’m doing so in the hope that it might help someone else to avoid this kind and depth of trauma.

label.evidence_quote: left feeling foolish for placing trust

All. I write to advise you to avoid Lister . avoid the head consultant James Nicopoullos. avoid Dr Marie Wren. But most importantly avoid Lister ! 1) Adequately read our file and historical information in preparation for the FET cycle.  The Lister team failed to learn from the basic progesterone blood test levels taken in June and failed to adapt the drugs protocol accordingly for the July for the FET, impairing our chance of success.

What makes the above even worsens that  In June when the Lister recommended a progesterone blood test pre the ERA biopsy, it came back at 0.6 ml.  Despite paying for this test The  insufficient reading was never identified - either The Lister team failed to read or failed to act on this blood test result.

This pattern of low progesterone levels, despite administering cyclogest, only came to the discussion after I had to probe the nurse team and Dr Bora (the day after the FET) as to why we were dealing with a surprise blood result.  At this point we’d undergone not one but two consultations with Marie Wren (all charged and paid for ) to strategically plan for the use of our three remaining embryos. There should have been no surprises at this point given the learnings from the ERA cycle. Furthermore, during this exchange Dr Bora described the 0.6ml reading as “the lowest reading she had ever seen” . Quite the statement from an experienced Doctor.

Dr Marie Wren advised us that Lister would review Their process here in light of our experience . Lister have clearly failed to follow a logical process which would be apparent to any reasonable mind . We do not believe Lister have amended anything to date .

text:
My partner and I went to The Evewell Clinic for secondary infertility and, now that our journey has ended, I feel compelled to share our mixed experience. We entered IVF knowing nothing and were completely unprepared for how painful and overwhelming it would be.

The nursing team was the highlight of the clinic—kind, patient, and genuinely supportive. They explained things clearly and offered emotional reassurance when we needed it most. Unfortunately, this level of care didn’t extend to the rest of the clinic. The administrative and finance staff often lacked empathy and organisation, and their communication felt cold and dismissive at times. For a process as emotionally heavy as IVF, this really mattered.

Dr. Eva was pleasant, but difficult to reach, and important information wasn’t communicated clearly. We often felt left in the dark, especially about risks, statistics, and what to expect. Messages went through a central team, and replies were slow or never came. This added a lot of unnecessary stress.

Costs were another major issue. We repeatedly asked for clarity but were only given a price list full of jargon we didn’t understand. No one explained what an average patient might expect to pay. We also later discovered we could have bought medication far cheaper elsewhere—something the clinic never mentioned, even though the difference was over £1,000 in our case. It left us feeling taken advantage of at a time when we were vulnerable.

Across two IVF cycles, we created 13 embryos and ended up with two viable ones after PGTA testing. Our first transfer failed. The second embryo was lost during thawing—something with a 99% success rate. We were devastated and unconvinced by the vague explanation of “it happens sometimes.” We are still convinced it was human error. After that, they suggested ovulation induction, which was poorly managed and far more expensive than implied.

What hurt most was the lack of guidance. We weren’t told early on that, at our age, donor eggs might be a realistic consideration—information that could have helped us prepare emotionally. They only discussed it after our second failure, and even admitted they lose patients because they don’t offer donor services. It felt like information was withheld simply because it didn’t benefit the clinic.

By the end, we felt abandoned. No aftercare, no follow‑up, no support—just silence. Not even a sales pitch. After everything we went through, that was incredibly painful.

I’m sure there are better clinics out there, and I hope others have a kinder, more transparent experience than we did.

label.evidence_quote: no follow up after care
