text:
Update:

I found the clinic through doing research and trusted them because of this and the fact that they are on Harley Street and you’d expect a better service from a clinic on “Harley street”

The biggest mistake I made was going to this clinic. On the initial consultation i told the nurse Georgina that I was adamant doing the procedure in March, because this was the only month I could do it( I travel a lot for work). However, I had a business trip when I was meant to get my period and mentioned to Georgina I would need to delay the period in order to start the process when I returned. I was told this was no problem and that I can take period blockers which would delay my period and I could start the egg freezing process when I was back.

I was given a prescription for norethisterone and told to take it once a day. This is what
I did - I was also told by a friend that she was on the same pills and was told to take 3 times a day, which confused me and I called the clinic and asked my coordinator Orla to confirm how many times a day I should be taking the medication, to which she said once a day.

Unfortunately, on the 24th of February I got my period, and when I checked the box of the medication it said I was meant to be taking the medication 3 times a day. When I called the clinic up I had to wait to then find out that they made a mistake and I was meant to be taking 2-3 pills a day. As you can imagine I was very upset and reminded them that this was the only month I could have done this procedure. At this point, I was put in contact with a doctor from the clinic, who ensured me that I could still go ahead with the egg freezing, even though my period started. The nurse said that she did treatment on people that don’t even get a period or patients that have cancer. Being compared to a patient with cancer doesn’t
Make me feel good. I am healthy and young I should not be compared to a patient with cancer.

To cut the story short, I could not do the treatment when I wanted to, as it was too late. I wasted money and time to attend all my appointments to then be misinformed about how to take medication. You would expect a fertility clinic to know the correct dosage. Do not compare your patients to others, educate your employees and stop taking advantage. Do not choose this clinic. They do not know what they are doing. All they care about is money, the communication is terrible. They are more expensive than other clinics. They will leave you in the dark and they only time you will have good communication is when they are taking payment from you.

They say they care about mental health but they do not, I am now not able to do the egg freezing until the end of the year, as I do not have the time. I made this all very clear when I first contacted the clinic. I was told “sorry it is human error”. I do not expect this human error from a “leading” woman’s clinic in Harley Street. This clinic chargers more than others, I would advice not choosing this clinic if you want to be taken care of, look elsewhere, into a smaller a smaller clinic that value their patients more. At this clinic you are just a number, they want your money and they don’t even know the ins and outs of treatment. I ended up having to google how many times a day I should be taking medication instead of being informed from either the nurse or my co ordinator.

I hope no other woman will have to go through what I went through.

label.evidence_quote: only month I could do it

text:
I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding one of your staff members, Miss Khadeejah (please note that the spelling of her name may not be entirely accurate). I have just experienced one of the rudest, most unprofessional, and least helpful phone conversations I have encountered in recent years. I feel compelled to bring this to your attention due to the seriousness of the matter. Below are the main issues I encountered:
1：Call Termination:
While our conversation was still ongoing and I was trying to send her screenshots showing the issues I was having with filling out the form, she abruptly hung up the phone. This was not due to a dropped call or technical issue—it was clear she intentionally ended the call.:
2：Lack of Willingness to Help:
When I asked if my previous appointment could be retained, she bluntly told me it could not. However, when I later spoke to another one of your staff members, Rebekah, she was extremely helpful and professional and was able to retain my previous appointment. It was clear that Khadeejah simply did not want to assist me and was unwilling even to check the system on my behalf.
3：Refusal to Help with Rebooking:
After informing me that my previous appointment had been cancelled, I asked Khadeejah to help me rebook a new one. She refused, stating that I must first complete the “Change of Details” form. However, I was experiencing technical issues editing the form and needed support. Instead of offering any help, she insisted she could not assist and refused to help me secure a new date.
The above are just a few of the issues from our call, not the entirety of the conversation. Throughout the call, her tone and attitude were consistently unfriendly and dismissive. I sincerely hope your organization can investigate this incident. If your calls are recorded, I urge you to review the recording of our conversation.
Please understand that I have no intention to defame Miss Khadeejah—I do not know her personally, and we have no prior history. However, her conduct today was shockingly unprofessional and left us at LWC extremely disappointed. This is especially disheartening given that I am a returning client; in fact, we had some tests done at your clinic as far back as 2018.
I hope you take this complaint seriously and take appropriate steps to ensure no other clients are treated in the same way.

label.evidence_quote: rudest, most unprofessional

text: Words can't express the regret I feel having made the choice to work with this clinic and place my trust in them. Having moved on and shared my experience with other professionals I am validated by their universal support and agreement, that serious missteps were taken while I was a patient.

label.evidence_quote: serious missteps were taken

text: I went through two rounds of IVF with LWC. The nurses at the Brentwood clinic and at Harley Street were nice. Unfortunately Dr Kovats at the Brentwood clinic has no bedside manner, I found her to be rude, she actually laughed at me down the phone once and told me to lose weight. I asked to not speak to her following that incident. Following the second round of IVF, which failed, I was not contacted for approx two weeks. Only then to receive an email to state, sorry the doctor has only just informed us what happened. In that time the app kept sending me lovely reminder messages to take my medication for the embryo that I didn’t have. The fact that they can’t even communicate between themselves shows how disorganised they are. The after care is shocking. Don’t waste your time and money with LWC, I wish I hadn’t. There is a serious issue with the duty of care to their patients, considering the vulnerability of people going through fertility issues and the large amounts of money they are happy to take off you. I once cried while talking to one of the nurses as I wasn’t getting very far with follicle growth, next time I spoke to the doctor, she made me feel bad about that and suggested counselling, she said she would send me the details and never did. I’m disappointed and disgusted with how I have been treated. You just feel like a number being dealt with. Good luck and go elsewhere!

label.evidence_quote: not contacted for approx two weeks

text:
DO NOT use the London Women's Clinic! We are absolutely disgusted with them. They lost our personal information (ID documents and blood test results); they called my partner by the wrong name in person and via email (how can we be sure this mix up of names didn’t happen in the laboratory?); they have lied in our notes about what was said in meeetings; they failed to provide medical information, until our 3rd complaint; they continued to request tests at the very last minute (probably so we didn't have a chance to have them completed elsewhere); they requested over £3,000 payment for tests while I was at a scan, in reception (we had never been informed that this was to be paid up front as we didn't know until the day of transfer if it was needed), then continued to chase me, again in reception, at every scan; calls were not returned, emails not responded to, the list goes on. If it was just 1 thing, we probably would have laughed it off. But it was just 1 thing after another. Their organisation, empathy and professionalism is appalling. Just please, if you want a stress free treatment, avoid them. I don't want anyone to have to go through what we went through, still are 7 months later. They failed us entirely. The theme in the complaint responses was "we apologise for any distress caused, but..." followed by an excuse. Is that really an apology? "It was an oversight". These "oversights" should not continue to happen, surely? This has truly upset and angered us. The just don't care. It was all completely unnecessary and could have been avoided. This review doesn't even warrant a 1* as it suggests there was some level of service. There was not. No doubt any response to this review will be full of yet more lies. There's nothing more that can be said or done to put any of this right, the mental and physical damage has been done. It's broken our hearts. I do just hope that we have been the only 1s to suffer like this as I wouldn't wish this on anyone.

UPDATED: LWC did NOT complete a "thorough" investigation. In fact HFEA requested that LWC review the complaint because they hadn't responded fully. I did not disclose in my review that I had reported it to HFEA, so they're sharing information (now publicly) that is not theirs to share. However, for further clarification, HFEA responded to say "unlike some other healthcare bodies, we do not have a specific statutory duty to investigate patient complaints". Not really a "thorough" investigation is it?

I had hoped that our experience was not the norm (as explained in my review, I would not wish this on anyone) but it seems that we are not the only ones to have suffered, with much worse things happening. It has now come to light that they hide/delete/block any negative feedback because it "may cause distress to other patients". We felt we had done our due diligence in finding the right clinic for us - checking reviews etc. If the experiences that are now coming to light were not hidden by the clinic, we would never, ever have put our trust in them.

We did/do not have any specific "needs" that needed to be accommodated other than finding a clinic that cares about their patients/treats them with dignity and respect, which I would expect every patient has.

label.evidence_quote: lost our personal information

text:
It has taken me some time to post this but after being chased ,a few years after the event, to sign forms for embryo destruction, I am once again reminded how poor their care was.

The consultant at the time didn't really answer complex questions. Everything was simplified, ie My stats/AMH etc were good, it was all good etc.

No one advised me of alternatives, range of opinion, risks and when i questioned why we were continuing as the stats did in fact not stack up, i was ignored and simply told to continue as it all looked fine. Not the case at all sadly.

I was also held to ransom when they realised I had been given the wrong costings advice. I had to pay the difference or else they would not release my embryos/test them.
In the end, I complained. Got a partial refund (try negotiating when you are without your medical notes/grieving) as I decided I didn't have the strength to take them to court.

I obtained my notes after the event. Zero oversight by a consultant during the process was noted in the notes. It confirmed my suspicion that it was very much a conveyor belt approach.

I know it will all depend what people's circumstances are, who your consultant is. All i will say is that we switched clinic and it was a completely different story. Just have your wits about you and make sure you have everything in writing.

label.evidence_quote: No one advised me of alternatives

text:
I would describe LWC as a poor, substandard and lazy medical institution. The comments below are my honestly held, personal opinions based on my experience having IVF at the LWC in 2023. I could not go into the detail I wanted due to limited word count unfortunately.

I am 34 and had an admittedly difficult diagnosis of very low AMH, which was weaponised to repeatedly tell me that IVF would be a hopeless endeavour and strongly push towards [the much more expensive] egg donation route. The IVF protocol I was put on was incredibly basic - I felt there was a lack of any personalised treatment which could have better served me and my diagnosis.

There is no routine monitoring of blood work at all throughout an IVF cycle at LWC; this is truly baffling when considering IVF is a hormonal based treatment and the only way to assess its efficacy is to consistently monitor blood hormone levels. I found this to be lazy and an excuse to do the bare minimum.

I had one dominant follicle which burst. LWC brushed this off as "bad luck" as opposed to considering the impact of the extremely high stimulation I was placed on coupled with the lack of any proper monitoring blood work. In a meeting just after this news was broken to me, I was treated insensitively; I felt the doctor used this cycle failure to justify his own prediction of a poor outcome, as opposed to reflecting as to how a better outcome could be achieved in the future. I found this to be highly disappointing and again, lazy. I was again pushed to switch to egg donation.

I was then ignored and forgotten about - literally. I was promised a revised treatment plan and I heard nothing back. After 25 days, I contacted LWC to ask what was happening. I had simply been forgotten. This behaviour does not strike me as a clinic who truly cares about its patients. When the treatment plan arrived, it was extremely basic and contained no information as to how it would remedy the previous failures. I found this to be lazy and highly indicative of poor patient care.

The administrative side of LWC was chaotic; it adds rather than detracts from the stress you are under. Letters were sent after many weeks and only after chasing - I felt they were curt, poorly written and contained limited helpful medical advice. Paperwork was regularly not in order.

I felt something was not right, the doctor had no faith in me, he seemed unmotivated in helping me achieve a successful outcome - seemingly contented with his diagnosis of me being bound to fail. The doctor also strongly suggested that no other clinic would be able to achieve success either, telling me verbatim: "if anyone tells you that they can collect more than one egg from you they are lying".

Despite the LWC's indication that I was a hopeless case which no fertility clinic could help, I decided to seek a second opinion. After 2 rounds of IVF at another clinic, we made 6 embryos and I am now 4.5 months pregnant. The LWC's dogmatic and rigid assessment that I would was an unsuitable candidate for IVF was simply medically incorrect. Had I stayed at LWC, I would have never had the chance to have my own children - and they sought to convince me this was an inevitability.

I formally complained to the LWC; they made partial admissions to "poor communication" but nothing further. My complaint was poorly handled, with many points I raised being ignored. Before I escalated the complaint to the regulator, I provided the LWC with a further chance to reply more fully as a courtesy - they declined. This struck me as a business who did not care what their patients thought of them, nor what the regulator would think of them.

LWC not only took our hard earned life savings, but something much, much more valuable - our time. I felt denigrated and unsupported during one of the most vulnerable times of my life.

Please, please carefully consider whether this clinic is for you - particularly if you have a more complex infertility diagnosis. You deserve to have the best treatment possible during what is probably one of the most difficult times of your life.

label.evidence_quote: IVF would be a hopeless endeavour

text:
Very disappointed with my experience.

After going through two full cycles at this clinic, I feel increasingly let down rather than supported. What I expected was professional guidance, care, and a genuine willingness to help patients navigate such an important and emotional journey. Unfortunately, that was not what I experienced.

Instead, I often felt that the process was treated as routine rather than personalised. I did not feel there was enough responsibility taken or proactive effort to truly understand or improve my situation. Over time, this has not only been disappointing, but has also affected my confidence and trust — not just in this clinic, but more broadly in fertility services and even parts of the UK healthcare system.

For something as sensitive and significant as fertility treatment, patients need more than just procedures — they need care, attention, and a sense that the doctor is truly working with them to find solutions.

Sadly, I did not feel that here, and based on my experience, I would not recommend this clinic.

label.evidence_quote: treated as routine rather than personalised

text:
I spent a long time thinking about whether to write this review, and then I realised - some people only have one chance and one pot of money...
My Story: I began my fertility journey at LWC London Bridge. Everything seemed fine at first - I started the injections, followed the protocol, and was preparing for egg collection. But the day before the procedure, a scan revealed that my eggs had already started releasing. This meant no eggs could be collected.
Later, I learned that this could have been prevented with frequent blood tests to monitor my hormone levels - something LWC didn’t do. Instead, I was told this happens to “1 in 100 people.” But had I been monitored properly, it likely wouldn’t have happened at all. The cost for this round set us back approx £6k, it felt difficult at the time too, we had to wait weeks to go again.
Round Two: Determined to try again, I went ahead with a second round at LWC. This time, the doctors were understandably cautious, but it resulted in only seven eggs collected, leading to two low-grade embryos and no baby. By this point, I had spent approx £18,000, and I was left feeling like my egg reserve was low and my body wasn’t responding well.
While the nurses at LWC are lovely and genuinely want the best for patients, the monitoring simply wasn’t tailored or thorough enough.
The Turning Point: We done some research and made the decision to switch to ARGC, (this clinic has one of highest success rates in the UK I believe alongside the Evewell clinic) . On my first round there, 23 eggs were collected - yep, 23. In hindsight, my struggles at LWC weren’t due to poor egg reserve or my body not responding. The problem was inadequate monitoring. With closer tracking, including more frequent blood tests, my body responded just fine. Thanks to the other clinic we now have a beautiful baby and 5 good grade embryos in the freezer.
My Advice: If you have unlimited resources and can afford multiple rounds, LWC may be an option. But if you have only one shot - do your research. Ask to see their success rates. In my experience, they are lower than other clinics, and proper monitoring could make all the difference in your journey. I’m sorry LWC for having to write this review, however this is the truth, and people struggling to have a baby or start a family haven’t got endless pots of money, I hope you can understand.

label.evidence_quote: eggs had already started releasing

text:
We are deeply dissatisfied with some parts of the treatment process. Following our initial consultation there was no further information mentioned to us about the Fibroid that was found in the initial scan, we were informed that we would be updated if there were any concerns or any further procedures that would need to be considered or happen as part of IVF treatment.

Then I was told to go into the clinic for the embryo transfer, we were told this would be a quick procedure and my partner wouldn’t need me to accompany me.

We then received a message on the app that partner should accompany and was then told that we should have been informed this previously,however it was too late for my partner to be able to attend by this point.

At the embryo transfer appointment I was then told that I can have an option to remove Fibroid which may increase chances of pregnancy, but this would include further costs, time, and potential risks of freezing embryos. This had not been mentioned to us at all since the initial consultation and was a shock to be given a choice to make on the day of the embryo. transfer that I was not expecting at all.   I was informed that I had to make the decision on the same day.

This was extremely stressful situation to be in without any prior conversations about this at all since the initial consultation. This was very distressing decision to make at this point.

I was also told that this wasn’t managed earlier as staff didn’t expect the embryos to be healthy or good. This again is extremely upsetting to hear on the day of the embryo transfer, and we are dissatisfied that this was not discussed with us previously.

I could’ve arranged the Fibroid procedure at any time since our initial consultation to not cause so much distress and challenging decision to make on the day of the embryo transfer.

For the money that we spent, £9000 we are really dissatisfied with this private service and expected better care on this journey.

When we complained they replied that It was unclear if we had been told this information after we had informed them that we hadn’t. We wouldn’t trust this clinic anymore and have gone somewhere else for further treatment.

text:
Please take the negative reviews seriously - do not go to this clinic. Writing down our experience to hopefully help couples:
We are both healthy adults in our 30s who wanted to do basic checks, just to catch any issue early (if there was one). What subsequently played out at the clinic was ridiculous.
First, the clinic is dirty and cramped. The moment you enter it does not feel like a premium and well run organisation.
For the female internal examination, the lady doing the test was extremely rude and dismissive to my partner when she requested me to be in the room to accompany her. She said "there's no space here, you are wasting my time". Unacceptable.
Once all testing was complete, things only got worse. Getting an appointment with Dr Venkat to receive the results was difficult.
At the appointment she started with "Good news" but then tacked on the fact that it would be very difficult for us to conceive naturally. She cited specifics. She seemed genuine. She suggested we go straight to IVF. After her call we received follow up emails from the clinic further perpetuating this.
However, when we looked at the actual test paperwork there was NO mention of the conditions she had mentioned.
We went for a second opinion with a leading OBGYN who reviewed the test results, did a scan, and categorically told us that there were no issues at all and we should have no problem conceiving. She was absolutely right: 3 months later we were pregnant and we now have a healthy child.
On reflection, I believe that DR Venkat (who I believe owns the clinic) and her team are purposefully pushing healthy patients to do IVF to boost their success statistics. The callousness of doing something like this for financial gain is insane, but if you read other reviews you can see a pattern. I am writing this review to encourage people to go elsewhere where they will get the care they deserve and be treated honestly.

label.evidence_quote: the clinic is dirty and cramped

text:
My husband and I both had tests done at HSFC, which was professional and we then had a video call to go through our results.

The results implied that my husband was infertile but at no point was it suggested we retake the tests or that there were any options other than IVF.

We subsequently went to The Lister Hospital for a second opinion and my husband had the tests redone and it transpires that my husband was not infertile. The Dr at The Lister explained to us that results can be skewed by viruses/ dehydration etc. so you should always retake the tests. This was never suggested to us by HSFC and the call was a hard IVF with no thorough explanation of what was actually wrong with my husband's results.

The Dr at The Lister asked me my results and with his advice, we were pregnant within a month and now have a healthy 6 month old.

I'm a pragmatic individual but it has taken me over a year to write this review. My advice would purely be that if you get negative results, get a second opinion.

label.evidence_quote: no retake suggested

text:
I would first like to state that they will respond to this saying none of it is true. I promise you this is 100% true and we just don’t want anyone else to go through the utter trauma and expense that we experienced. From our first visit we should have seen the warning signs about the clinic but from having nothing to compare it to it’s so hard to navigate this system. I was made to sob on our first visit due to the delivery of news about my situation. In hindsight I’m convinced this is a tactic used by the clinic to physiologically make you think that you really need them. I think this because as my treatment continued and I was too far in to back out I saw many other girls sobbing like I had that day. The rudeness of the staff is consistent throughout the entire clinic. Training in customer care seems to not happen at all and makes for a trying time when you are so emotionally and financially invested. The whole circumstances were made even worse by the fact that nothing in the clinic is computerised. All notes are physically written and there is only one file (very dangerous in terms of the event of a fire etc) it also means no one has any idea what’s going on. Be aware if you choose this clinic you better stay on top of your treatment as they won’t. A quick note on facilities…they are shocking. When you come to having and surgery/transfers or eggs collected good luck!! The room in the basement is AWFUL. It’s dirty and dank. There isn’t enough equipment for the amount of patients, they have to keep unplugging the heart rate monitor (without cleaning in-between) to put on each patient whilst being monitored. I was absolutely SHOCKED. The only toilet that is available to use is in the staff cloakroom full of old stinky shoes. Not the best when you just had very intimate surgery.
My story took a dark turn when we came to having our embryo transfer. I was advised on many medications that I needed to take to get my body ready for this. Now bearing in my at points I was taking up to 10 - 15 different medications daily and it’s not my job to understand exactly what every one of those does. You are putting your trust in the Drs at this stage. In my case Dr Venkat. I was without knowing what it was put onto a high dosage of prednisone (a very strong steroid). I understand that I may have needed this to make my treatment successful and I’m not opposed to that. I just had any issue with the fact that not at any point did anyone explain the side effects and possibilities from taking this. It’s very very likely to cause very high blood sugar levels. Which in my case it did and wasn’t discovered until I was at hospital. Not once was I advised this could be an issue. They didn’t advise me to monitor my sugar levels or let my GP know that I was taking this so they could monitor it. Nor did they check my sugar levels before prescribing this drug to me!! The hospital (Kings College London) was absolutely SHOCKED at this! They said this is absolutely medically negligent. For the first 3 months of my pregnancy I was walking around with sky high glucose levels which puts you in grave danger of a miscarriage.
When I complained to the clinic rather than handling this situation like adults Dr Venkat just threw her toys out of the pram and refused to treat me further leaving me in a very frightening situation at 3 months pregnant with nowhere else to go. They also plain face lied that I had been made aware of the situation. I honestly couldn’t believe it. This is why we are currently seeking advice to take this further. But in the meantime I implore anyone looking to seek treatment, PLEASE GO ELSEWHERE. Don’t make the same mistake that we did. There is so much more to say but it’s impossible to say it all here. It was 1.5 years of trauma. I’d be more than happy to contact anyone who needs further advice or who would like to join forces in stopping this clinic from further treating people like this. Please look me up on social media and I will reply. Samantha Clare Kininmonth.

label.evidence_quote: made to sob

text:
Whilst we are now pregnant, I would advise everyone to use any other fertility clinic than Harley Street Fertility Clinic (HSFC).

Please read all other 1 and 2 star reviews - we didn't and truly regret it now.  All these poor reviews give a true and fair representation as to your journey with this clinic.

Total lack of communication, unexpected bills every step of the way (including one bill on the day of £1950 that we weren't told about!).  This clinic is solely there to earn as much money as possible out of each patient.

Most concerning was that at the end of February 2025, unbeknownst to us, amongst the drugs that had been instructed to take for the fertility treatment, HFSC included a steroid - Prednisone.  HSFC did not highlight that this was a steroid or the reason why it was prescribed; it was included with the other supplements we were instructed to take for the fertility treatment.  We now aware that this steroid is extremely likely to cause hypoglycemia especially in the size of doses given.  This led to my partner being hyperglycemic for 3 months, and not finding this out until the first midwife appointment.  This puts the person at a high risk of miscarriage and could have been very detrimental to either Mother or Babies health.

We highlighted the above to HSFC in the form of an official complaint, and we're simply told all medical guidelines were met during their investigation.

All of the other fertility clinics have better reviews than HSFC - my advise would be to take note of that.

label.evidence_quote: unexpected bills

text:
Highly advise against using this clinic. Please read the multitude of 1 and 2 star reviews. The reviews and ratings are a true reflection of their complete lack of professionalism. Please also notice that the most recent “positive” reviews are vague and have the exact same number of reactions - which, if you read to the end of the review, is clearly an attempt to course correct their negative reviews.

We had rescheduled our appointment with the clinic and upon arriving, not only was the receptionist rude saying we were late for our appointment which turned out to be a scheduling error on their part as we had email proof, but she then proceeded to give an excuse saying she had called us - which she would have called 30 minutes after the time they had scheduled us for. After realizing their error, she just said to go “empty your bladder” without specifying which of us or even where the restroom is.

She then tells us that we need to wait an additional 45 minutes for our appointment because the doctor and her manager are in a meeting as a result of their scheduling error. When I told her this was there fault, she goes back to the “I tried to call you” even though that would have been 30 minutes after what they had scheduled in error, so another demonstration of their incompetence. All in all, a waste of time.

Please run away from this clinic - I regret having even done the consultation here since we’re now locked in to doing the rest of the testing. If you are someone that has a schedule or other commitments to abide to that you need to schedule around, this is not the right clinic for you.

Pick any other clinic but this one.

Amending this review following our last and final call with Dr Venkat to review our results. She showed up 10 minutes late to our virtual appointment and said she is only human and the other couple were upset and continued to ask questions which is why she was late. This is another example of how the clinic does not respect the patients’ time - if they anticipate couples will have questions, they should schedule longer appointments and not put the onus for the delay on the next patient. Not only that, but they did not even have my husband’s results to review on the call which demonstrates the level of disorganization. After we waited for someone to bring the results up to review, we expressed that we would no longer be patients at this clinic. Dr Venkat asked why and when we begun to explain our experience, she raised her voice at us and refused to let us speak. She said we caused enough damage to her clinic and that’s why they have had no customers in 2 weeks. When we tried to speak, she continued to cut us off. When we said it was inappropriate that she was yelling at us or to say that we caused damage to our clinic, she proceeded to say she was not yelling and that she didn’t say we caused damage, but our 1 star review did, which meant she was clearly already aware of our experience.

The clinic’s manager had previously called us to try to see how we could remedy the experience and not post a 1 star review, but I do not feel that it would be appropriate to any other person to not be aware of the terrible experience we had prior to investing in any type of fertility treatment. This clinic is extremely unprofessional. As much as I’d like to think it was just the receptionist and scheduling that went awry, the primary doctor herself proved that the clinic is systemically unprofessional.

Ultimately, it is your choice where you choose to invest your money and time for your fertility consultations and treatment. I cannot, in my good conscience, recommend this clinic to anyone. We will be sharing our feedback to HFEA following this last, and thankfully final, experience.

label.evidence_quote: rescheduled our appointment

text:
I don’t think words or a 1* review will ever be enough to truly discourage anyone thinking of undergoing any fertility services from HSFC to please DON’T DO it. They put myself and my husband through 5 months of nothing but stress, disappointment and heartache that I will never get back, let alone £1000s. My final straw was when I ended up having to see an emergency GP followed by an urgent referral to a gynaecologist through the NHS and being told my treatment and advice from the clinic was completely negligent and my condition was caused by them! Not a single nurse or consultant bothered putting my care first and instead prioritised giving me advice that only favoured me hopefully getting pregnant and getting their success rates up. They then had the cheek to ask me if I wanted to go in and PAY to be examined by their consultant which blew my mind as this was something that should have happened before I had to receive NHS treatment. Add this to the months of no communication from nurses, having to chase multiple times to get hold of anyone, not being given enough medication in cycles (which I realised and flagged myself) resulting in me having to find a local pharmacist to accept an emailed prescription from them in order to get hold of what I needed in time, turning up to appointments (which took me 2 hours to get to) and being told the nurse didn’t book me in, travelling home (for 2 hours) after an appointment and being called 4 hours later to say please come back you need more medication, to turning up to collect medication and being told you only need 1 dose but we sell it in doses of 4 so you need to pay x4 the amount of what you actually need and just throw the rest away. The nurses have no compassion whatsoever and every time you go in or call you speak to someone new. There is nothing personal or supportive about your experience with them. The list could go on and on and considering I used this clinic to help me conceive after being unsuccessful, they caused me more stress than I ever could have imagined and I never want anyone else to experience it. Whatever response they reply to this please ignore. I asked for compensation and they said they felt they were “responsive to all concerns raised”. How they think any of the above was responsive let alone acceptable sums them up!

IN RESPONSE TO YOUR REPLY HSFC:

You never asked me for a letter from another medical professional and if you had I would have sent it to you, especially if this was what was needed as "part of the investigation into my complaint." You also never once told me that an examination by one of your consultants would be at no cost. But to be honest, this is all absolutely NOT the point. The point of my review and complaint against you was that this was something you should have done when I came to the clinic initially with heavy bleeding, not offered to me AFTER I had already done via the NHS because your clinic wouldn't. I am complaining about the lack of duty of care you gave me when you should have, not afterwards. So your point is completely void.

I would love to know what appropriate corrective actions you took because the service didn't change until the day I walked away from the clinic?

Regarding your last point, I absolutely can provide you with a report from the Gynaecology Consultant I saw stating what it was and that it was caused by what you were giving me. But what would I even gain from doing that? You've already told me you won't be compensating me so what is the point?

I'm also not quite sure what you mean by we want to clarify that some of the claims in your review do not align with our records? I wrote you an email listing every single complaint in it which you came back to me to say you won't be doing anything about? So that's also completely false!

label.evidence_quote: treatment was com

text:
I am absolutely appalled by the disgraceful and negligent treatment my sister endured at this clinic. I was present for many of her phone interactions with the staff, and the experience has been nothing short of a nightmare. To make matters worse, the clinic responded to her recent review with blatant lies about her treatment and claims, so I’m writing this to set the record straight and bring some honesty to a place that clearly lacks it.

This clinic has failed, on every level, to show the basic professionalism and compassion that anyone would expect from a facility handling such delicate, personal issues. They seem more interested in racking up ‘successful’ pregnancy numbers and lining their pockets than caring for the actual well-being of their patients. My sister was told to keep ‘trying’ despite consistent bleeding - advice that any responsible clinic would recognise as a cause of concern. It was only after an emergency GP appointment that we learned just how reckless and harmful their approach had been and that the ‘treatments’ the told her to keep taking were actually causing her symptoms and a mini procedure was needed. The lack of basic care and concern is honestly beyond words.

I want to emphasise that this is not a complaint I make lightly and it’s not usually in my nature. I’m writing this because people deserve to know the risks of entrusting their care to this clinic. I value honesty, and I want to protect others from the emotional and physical harm my sister has suffered. I plan to bring this issue to several support groups as well, to make sure no one else has to endure the same nightmare.

I hope this clinic reflects on this and puts their time and energy into prioritising the lives and well-being of its patients over profit and replying to google reviews with false information. No family should ever have to go through what we have because of their reckless, careless treatment.

text:
A pretty traumatic experience especially for people going through a very challenging period of their life:
1) HSFC (especially the coordinators/front of house team) did not seem to know what was going on. They always required me (as the patient) to follow up even though they said they would and they kept referring me to other "colleagues", so I was bounced around from team to team with no clear answer. When I asked to speak to my Doctor (as I was hoping that the doctor would be my single source of truth), they said I would have to pay another £240 for a consultation (ridiculous given that I had already paid a fairly substantial amount to even get that far and had not received much in return!) Definitely feels very salesy and pushy.
2) I felt the medical "advice/recommendation" was misleading and scared me into pursuing a treatment with the clinic. It was only after going to a SEPARATE clinic and paying a SEPARATE consultation fee, that I realised that I was only told half the story. I'd recommend anyone who visits ANY fertility clinic to first read the book: It Starts with the Egg by Rebecca Fett. It is in the fertility clinic's interest to get you to do a treatment and so it's good to get some impartial advice beforehand so please do your research.
3) This isn't exclusive to this clinic but to almost all the fertility clinics out there - I am disappointed that the costs for treatments aren't more clearly documented, shared and most importantly regulated. Sure there's a price list which they can distribute (with large price ranges and names of complicated drug names etc) and I do appreciate that treatments will vary from person to person. BUT often, it feels like fertility clinics will only share the prices to your personalised treatment plan once you're already quite far invested into the process and so will have to pay the ad-hoc/hidden costs that they sometimes spring on you throughout the process. Furthermore, informing patients of the costs so late on does not allow patients to do proper cost-comparisons at the beginning and so creates this horrible power dynamic. I don't know what the solution to this fertility-clinic-collusion is other than maybe for there to be stricter price regulations and for clinics to create personalised treatment plans much earlier than they currently do.

I was with Harley Street Fertility clinic for a year and had undergone multiple IVF cycles. To say that it was a positive experience would be a lie. Often the majority of appointments running late and the doctors treated you more like a number rather than a person. Our first cycle I had my beta blood work and progesterone blood work done early on Saturday morning and was called an hour before close to pick up more progesterone. My husband rushed to get there (we live outside of London) and asked for the needles (he meant injections). The receptionist then handed him a SEALED bag and off he went home. When we got home, I went to take my injection and found I only had syringes in the bag. The doctor knew I was coming to get my progesterone prescription, because they had put me on a higher dosage. I then called their out of hours number and talked to Dr Venkat. She told me that she couldn't do anything for me, it was my husband's fault for asking for needles and that the nurses had asked him if he meant the medication (this was a lie). I then asked her if she could send the prescription to a chemist for me and she said no. When I asked her what would happen to my baby, she said that my beta was low anyways and that without the progesterone I could lose the baby. I cried to her on the phone, this was my first positive pregnancy test in 5 years. I begged her to not let me lose the baby because of this. She told me to not put this on her and she said 'any loss is your husband's fault'. She then hung up the phone on me. An hour later she called me back and agreed to send in a prescription.
After that I did a further 2 rounds of IVF with them, I felt trapped there as I had 12 frozen embryos and didn't want to discard them.  After each failed cycle, it appeared to me that the treatment provided contradicted my issues that caused my infertility. For example, I have elevated killer cells, but was given a scratch and transferred 2 embryos at a time. This would cause more killer cells to attack the cervix. When I suggested this could be why the transfer wasn't successful in my follow up with the Dr,  the dr acknowledged this could have had an impact and that we could do it differently the following time. Surely the doctor would have considered this before my transfer, especially since they are the experts?  After having various tests, treatments and procedures done, I felt deflated and gave up.

This story has a happy ending, 3 years later without any IVF or medical support, I gave birth to a healthy baby boy. While I do not regret trying through IVF, I wish I took the time to research clinics carefully and hope sharing my experience will help people find the right clinic for them. X

clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)
text:
I am writing this review to warn others who may be considering CRGH. Unfortunately, my experience has been deeply disappointing and distressing.

1. Incorrect stimulation protocol (rookie mistake)
My consultant (Jara Ben Nagi) prescribed an inappropriate stimulation dose despite having full access to my previous IVF protocol. In my earlier cycle at another clinic, I was given 375 units, which resulted in 4 blastocysts.
At CRGH, I was initially prescribed 325 units, which was only increased to 375 units after 3 days, when it was already too late. This cycle resulted in just 2 blastocysts.
When I challenged my consultant, she admitted she could have prescribed a higher dose from the start. Correct stimulation dosing is basic IVF care, and this mistake should not have happened.

2. Lack of transparency during egg collection
Egg collection was performed by another consultant (Efsthatios Theodorou) . After the procedure, I was told that 6 mature eggs were collected, which was shocking given that 17 follicles were visible on ultrasound.
When I asked how many eggs were collected in total, both consultants said “all” — yet were unable to provide a number. To this day, I still do not know how many eggs were actually retrieved.
The explanation provided was confusing and inconsistent, and even the embryology team could not confirm the total number. This lack of transparency is extremely concerning, especially as my previous IVF cycle at another clinic resulted in 11 eggs collected, 10 of which were mature.

3. Poor clinical support during the implantation phase
I have an underactive thyroid and take levothyroxine, with regular monitoring through my GP. Before transfer, my TSH was below 2.5. After transfer, it increased to 3.3, which can negatively affect implantation.
I contacted the clinic in a state of genuine panic and shared my blood results via screenshots from my NHS app, explaining that an official GP letter would take time. I urgently asked whether my medication dose needed adjusting.
A nurse called Anna refused to escalate the issue due to the format of the results. Only after I was extremely distressed — shouting and close to tears — did she agree to ask a doctor for advice and promised a same-day call. I am still waiting for that call. Why my concern and extreme worry wasn’t priority but following a procedure to the point of absurdity is?

4. My Consultant left without notification
During this same call, I was told — for the first time — that my consultant had left the clinic. No one informed me of this beforehand, nor was I told who my new consultant is, despite the fact that I am still in the implantation phase. This is completely unacceptable.

5. Extremely expensive and not worth it
CRGH is a very expensive clinic, and based on my experience, it is absolutely not worth the cost. Poor communication, questionable clinical decisions, lack of transparency, and inadequate patient support made an already stressful IVF journey far worse.

Do not use this clinic.
If you are deciding between clinics or just starting your fertility journey, I strongly recommend looking elsewhere.

text:
I had a very disappointing experience at CRGH City Clinic, specifically with Dr. Javier Cordero. The ultrasound follicle tracking consultation lasted less than one minute, with no proper measurements taken — only vague and inconsistent follicle counts that were later changed without explanation.

To verify, I went to another clinic the next day, where I received a thorough and professional scan with completely different results.

On top of that, I paid for three blood test metrics but later discovered that only the AMH test was done — something the clinic never disclosed until I was contacted by their financial department.

While CRGH eventually refunded all my payments after I raised a formal complaint, the time and trust lost cannot be recovered. Worse, no one explained how they plan to address these serious quality issues.

I cannot recommend this clinic. The service I received was far below the standards expected from an accredited fertility clinic, and such carelessness can lead to serious consequences for patients seeking treatment.

clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)
text:
Edit (27.08.2023):
I'm editing in light of CRGH asking me to contact them. Don't be fooled by this token response. I tried to raise concerns  a few times and they had no interest in what I had to say. All they care about is making money. Our consultant had no interest in doing what was best for us. We were kept in the dark about treatment decisions (despite us asking) and were told we couldn't speak to a doctor whenever we asked to. I'm so angry and upset.

Original review:
I am writing this review as someone who had a few cycles at CRGH. The clinic caused me nothing but stress.  The main problems were finance, nursing and admin.

The finance department at CRGH are unprofessional, dishonest and incompetent. They caused me a significant amount of stress at a time when I was meant to be minimsing stress.

A few examples of what they did:
- Overcharged me many times (this was normal practice).
- Regularly and aggressively harassed me for payments I had already made.
- Charged me for procedures I did not have.
- Quoted me the most expensive prices for tests when cheaper options were available.
- Said I couldn't proceed with an appointment until I had paid money I did not even owe.
- Quoted me one price and invoiced for a higher price, saying the prices had gone up.
- Regularly sent me invoices late and expected payment within two days.
- Never apologised for their mistakes, despite making errors regularly. They sometimes even tried to gaslight me and pretend they hadn't made a mistake.
- Conveniently ignored my messages querying costs.

Please please check every single item on every single invoice. Do not give finance your credit card details and do not authorise them to take payments at their will. You will regret this.

CRGH are very opaque about their pricing and you end up paying A LOT more than you originally think you will.  There's always added extras that crop up.

I should not have had to spend my time arguing with finance on the phone and trying to get refunds or credits applied but that is what I had to do. There seems to be no standards in the finance team at all. They had a significant negative impact on my mental health. Hardly what you need when undergoing a stressful process.

Nursing staff will tell you that many patients complain about the finance team but they just wash their hands off it and do nothing to help. These nurses are regulated by the NMC. How does being complicit in this unethical practice fit with the NMC professional standards of practice? Being a nurse means behaving ethically and challenging poor practice.

You will see from many reviews that the finance team's unethical practice is commonplace.  However, CRGH do nothing about it. This makes me think that overcharging is deliberate practice (in the hope that patients will not notice).

Some of the nursing staff are abrupt, uncaring and rude. I have no idea why they're working in fertility given they have no empathy.  They were constantly making mistakes and never apologised or took accountability.  They are completely disorganised and I had to point out their mistakes several times (e.g. prescription errors, not knowing my treatment plan, not responding to portal messages). They also seemed uninterested in my questions and concerns. They act like patients are an inconvenience to them.

Your consultant does not oversee your treatment and decisions are made by different doctors each time you have a scan. What is the point of having a named consultant if they do not oversee your treatment? It left me feeling mistrustful, in the dark and confused. This is not ok for the amount of money I paid.

When I used to call up, I would invariably end up speaking to admin staff who were patronising, rude and obstructive. It's almost as if they are trying to put obstacles in the way.

CRGH have no interest in accepting feedback and learning from it. Whenever I raised concerns, nothing ever changed and I just got fobbed off or gaslit. They just close ranks and make out you are the problem. This speaks volumes about what they really think of patients.

label.evidence_quote: tried to raise concerns a few times

text:
We started treatment with CRGH with lots of hope after a failed cycle in NHS.  The doctor asked us to redo lots of test (though we had done this a year back) and we did everything and spent a fortune
hoping there will be some positive output.  After all the investigation tests and history of tests from NHS, the doctor prepared a worst plan for Egg collection and the medicines suggested is also without any logic.  The follicles were not not growing as expected and we asked the hospital to cancel the cycle as we did not expect many mature eggs (basic logic and we were correct, not sure what the doctors logic was).
The doctor did not cancel the cycle and that means we paid a fortune for treatment and then as expected, there was only 2 average Embryo freezed (in NHS cycle we had 4 atleast).  On the embryo transfer day, we asked for both the embryos to be transferred and the embryologists prepared only one and the excuse she gave was lame and she prepared one more in the last minute. When the doctor transferred the embryo itself, I knew this wont be successful as he itself was not confident
and the blabbering he did was a proof (the NHS embryo transfer was smooth, but unfortunately miscarried).

I did not even know the logic of CRGH asking us to do multiple Covid 19 test when we were not even visiting the hospital and there was no symptoms also.  Anyway, we lost around 20K GBP for a BFN and what we feel is CRGH is only trying to extract money and no service at all.

Update:  I expected this standard template reply from the hospital, however the whole process was messed up big time and nothing was done correctly except sending invoices / debiting money on time.

label.evidence_quote: doctor prepared a worst plan

clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)
text:
Although I always read reviews, this is one of the first I have written. I did a huge amount of research before I chose a clinic, reading various reviews from many places.

I chose CRGH because I met a consultant there who seemed professional and knowledgeable. I started the process and very soon I realised how impersonal and factory like the place was. Unfortunately they made a few mistakes with me and I started to loose trust. They accidentally changed my appointments without me knowing and changed my consultant in the middle saying I had requested it, which I had not. There were also various hidden costs, such as the price of a test does not include the results for which you have to pay another amount to receive! The finance team are very active. The consultant herself was cold and impersonal, and in one confidential phone meeting with her, which she insisted must be on loud speaker, she didn’t let me know that there were other staff in the room until I questioned it.

I kept going as I had already started and didn’t want to go elsewhere in the middle. Unfortunately in the middle I was diagnosed with a condition and the consultant Dr S actually shouted at me as to why I had not told her before and why I had ‘withheld’ information. I told her that I had no idea I had this condition and it had only come up in a recent blood test. She was abrupt and challenging and more concerned about legally covering herself then acknowledging that it may be a shock to me too that I had just found out I had this condition. Though she was knowledgeable and experienced, she was cold, unfriendly and robotic and made me feel awful.

I tried to speak to the manager and he was very defensive that I was questioning a Consultant and put down the phone on me, saying he did not need to speak to me if he didn’t know who I was. Suffice to say I would never go back there again. Several of the nurses there were lovely as were the friendly receptionists, but if the manager himself is so rude, then if there is an issue, there is simply no-one to speak to who cares.

I wish everyone all the best in their journey and hope you find the right clinic and consultant for you.

label.evidence_quote: changed my appointments without me knowing

clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)
text:
I had a negative experience and they were unwilling to address any of my concerns. Problems:
1:They don’t put anything in writing for you, (no appointment times, or notes from your scans, nothing tangible) so I had to take notes myself constantly. Even emails are electronically held in their system and you can only access them temporarily, then they disappear, so it’s hard to keep track of all the info you need to know.
2: There’s an awkward reception system where they take your photograph and there’s always a camera on you and yet it’s hard to get in through the main door from the high street
3: the consultant I met with has limited social skills. He puts his foot in it often with very sensitive topics. He only reviews your notes once the appointments have begun. He gives advice off the top of his head so you’re never sure whether it’s considered medical advice or a random idea he thinks might be fun to try.
4: sometimes I would have to call or email 3 times before someone would call me back
5: it’s very expensive and the costs are not clearly revealed until the invoices arrive
6: they prescribe medication that they will tell you is accessible in all pharmacies and health shops but then you will find the medication is actually only available directly from the clinic, at triple the cost
7: I was told the final and most important procedure - the egg collection - would be under general anaesthetic and pain free. Instead they changed their minds at the last minute so I was awake and in pain throughout the surgery. They did not offer additional pain relief at any point.
8: maybe all of the above is worth it because it is a success for some people and they are able to have children through this process. I don’t know if this is true though. It felt like a scam to me, because after all this, after many expensive months of scans and appointments and injections and attempts they did not manage to collect and freeze more than one egg. So overall had a very negative experience, and unsuccessful, and they were unwilling to address any of my post- procedure concerns.

label.evidence_quote: don’t put anything in writing for you

clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)
text:
I’ve been happy with the medical care I’ve received so far. The doctors and embryologists have been excellent, and I’ve felt well looked after on the clinical side. However, the administrative side of the clinic has been extremely frustrating and really detracts from the overall experience.

There are frequent administrative errors that you, as the patient, end up having to chase all while managing full-time work and the stress of IVF. Previously, the old portal system worked well, and messages were responded to quickly. Phone lines were accessible, and things felt manageable.

However, since the new system was introduced a month ago, things have gone significantly downhill. The portal is not user-friendly, messages sent through it are not received or responded to, and phone lines are so overwhelmed that I’ve waited 40–50 minutes multiple times without ever getting through. Emails are replied to, but often days later, which is unhelpful when you need to schedule time-sensitive scans or treatments.

I’ve raised my concerns via email a couple of times, but haven’t received a meaningful response. Most recently, a nurse failed to send a time-sensitive prescription, which required multiple follow-ups from me to resolve, without so much as an acknowledgment or apology.

In a recent nurse consultation I was reassured the portal is now working, but that just isn’t the case, unless they are just choosing to ignore messages that I have sent.

I’m already dreading having to contact CRGH to book in for a baseline scan. How am I meant to arrange something for the next 1-2 days when no one answers the phone, I can’t contact the nurses via portal and emails take at least 48 hours for a response. It’s an impossible system to navigate.

What’s most disappointing is the lack of communication. Given that the new system clearly isn’t working, there’s been no formal update or warning that portal messages are going unanswered. This level of disorganisation is simply not acceptable, especially considering the high costs of treatment.

To add to this, the finance team has made errors on contracts and charges in the past,
so invoices need to be checked carefully.

I understand that new systems can come with teething problems, but nearly a month on, there’s still no resolution, no timeframe for fixing the issues, and no accountability. Based solely on the administrative experience, I sadly cannot recommend the clinic at this time.

label.evidence_quote: frequent administrative errors

clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)
text:
We were discharged from the clinic with mixed feelings.

Negatives: My partner was sometimes treated in a way that made her feel stupid for asking simple questions, and we received conflicting information on several occasions. In one cycle, the wrong cycle’s notes were initially used, meaning we nearly skipped a cycle unnecessarily; we avoided this only through persistence. When problems arose, some staff focused on blaming colleagues rather than patient care. Telephone staff sometimes lacked basic awareness of the clinic’s own processes and did not take the time to read notes properly, which was extremely stressful when trying to get answers and book appointments at critical points in our cycles. Some nurses lacked compassion and sensitivity, leaving my partner in tears after two appointments. At certain points, senior staff admitted the clinic was overwhelmed with calls/emails, making it difficult to contact the clinic. Peaks and troughs happen, but continuing to advertise for new patients while being overwhelmed by existing ones felt like putting profit before people. Pharmasure’s delivery service is excellent (the pharmacy the clinic use), but their prices are extortionate: despite being owned by IBSA (the manufacturer of most of the prescribed drugs), we were quoted between 25–100% more than third-party suppliers for the same drugs. The process sometimes felt like a generic process rather than tailored diagnosis and treatment. Finance was too detached with invoices and chasers arriving at insensitive times. The clinic is busy and can feel like an IVF factory at times.

Positives: Some nurses - especially senior staff - were outstanding: kind, compassionate, responsive, and professional. We are particularly grateful to Jessica, Anca, Sue, and Libby, who went above and beyond. The lab staff were also empathetic, communicative, and professional. After we raised service issues, the clinic took steps to put things right, and we noticed a significant service improvement towards the end of our journey. Our consultant, Dr Wael Saab (one of two Dr Saabs at the clinic), was informative, listened to our questions, and provided good care. The portal resources were helpful. We became pregnant after two rounds of IVF (albeit it is early days), so the clinic delivered the desired outcome. We had confidence in the clinic via its results and reports from friends who are in the industry who vouched for the quality of the lab.

Verdict: We are grateful for the result, but there were too many avoidable service issues; I would be cautious recommending the clinic because it caused unnecessary stress at times, but we did get the desired result and I am aware that patients at competitor clinics report similar issues.

Some tips for other patients:

- Do pre-IVF blood tests early (as these can delay treatment).
- Start supplements early (ideally ~3 months before treatment). We took Ovum (women) and Proxeed (men).
- Order drugs early to avoid last minute ordering stress and cost.
- Use ASDA Pharmacy for IVF drugs: they offer IVF drugs at cost price, but do allow 3–5 days from order to collection (vs Pharmasure's overnight delivery service). Pls note ASDA require you to attend in person to order and collect with signed prescriptions + prescriptions need to be for whole boxes. We felt the extra hassle was worth the cost saving.
- Advocate for yourself: ask questions and escalate to senior staff if you are unsatisfied with the answers.
- Expect costs of ~£2–3k+ for testing, then £8k+ per cycle including drugs (2025 prices) - don't be caught out by the, much lower, headline price for a cycle that all clinics seem to use. The headline price does not include drugs, testing, and other standard procedures which are required.
- If male factor infertility is identified during initial testing, consider seeing a specialist andrologist/fertility urologist outside of the clinic - as fertility clinics generally seem to have more consultants with a gynaecology background, yet male factor infertility effects 50% of couples.

clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)
text:
We chose CRGH because it also conducted research.  We felt that this would mean they would be more update.  Unfortunately, it just meant that they had little 'bed side manner'.
I'll start off by saying that our two cycles were unsuccessful.  However, my negative opinion was formed during the first cycle.
Communication prior to implantation was excellent.
I was given a cervix relaxant several hours before implantation, there was no where comfortable to go through this, either the very small reception area or, as I chose, cured up in great pain on a chair in a corridor of the adjacent dental building.
I was not offered sedation for implantation, I struggle with smear tests but implantation was horrendous.
As is advised, I was expecting to lie on the trolley for a half an hour after implantation, but no, after two minutes I was sent elsewhere.
Second cycle, I thought I was pregnant, and their test confirmed this but at 7 weeks had a scan and they could not see anything.  Told that the better quality scan was not working.  Told to go back in a couple of weeks.  The feeling of being pregnant soon went.  Returned to clinic to be told very coldly that nothing there. I much later learned that I had probably had a chemical pregnancy.
For various reasons we chose not to do a third cycle with the final embryos.  After a couple of years of paying for them to be frozen we decided that we would donate them to anyone who needed embryos for pregnancy.  This was a complicated process.  We had to fill in many forms, including a heartfelt note to the 'future' child/ren at 18.  A couple of years after we were contacted by the clinic as they had omitted to do some essential medical tests.  When we went to the clinic again for an appointment, we were kept waiting a couple of hours, we didn't even have the appointment with the named person dealing with donation, but, in a tiny room.  All the forms had to be completed again.  On the floor of the 'office' were patient files, showing confidential information such as name and client number.  We were even left alone in the room with them.  We were expected to complete the forms in that cramped 'office'.  We didn't, we had to get out of there and do the forms justice.  We went to the nearby shopping plaza and did over a very very long coffee.
So for a couple of years, our embryos were not even available for another couple to use.  Heartbreaking.  Now, the regulations have changed and embryos need to be from a woman under 40 and who has had a live birth.  So now, my time with CRGH is finally coming to an end, after 10 years.

Would I have been happy if the IVF had been successful, yes of course.  Would I have still thought that the process at CRGH was poor, yes, absolutely.

label.evidence_quote: no where comfortable to go through this

text:
Medical negligence by Fertility Plus has caused harm to my wife’s health and negatively impacted our future fertility treatment.

Undergoing treatment at Fertility Plus has resulted in harm to my wife’s health and wellbeing due a serious failing in patient safety and care that led to her being given 2 months of the completely wrong medication.

My wife did hours of research into suitable fertility clinics and identified Fertility Plus as being the one best placed to help her given the issues we have been facing during our fertility journey.

We had an initial consultation with Mr Gudi who was very positive and felt that assisted conception would be best placed to help us at this stage so recommended that my wife takes Letrozole 5mg for a total of 3 months to help with ovulation so gave the initial course of this in a box.

My wife then went on to see Dr Nisha for her appointments and was given a paper bag that had strips of medication in it. Although these were meant to be more of the Letrozole, we discovered that she was actually given Levothyroxine 25mcg. This is an entirely different medication that is used to treat hypothyroidism; a condition my wife does not suffer from. This is a serious mistake as not only was she not given the medication she was recommended, but rather been taking a medication that is not needed and has subsequently affected her thyroid gland.

As a doctor myself, I am fully aware of the issues taking medication that you do not need can have on the body and as well as having taken this wrongly given medication for two months; she has also experienced side effects. Her menstrual cycle has become irregular; something that she has never experienced before in her life. This not only impacts our future fertility treatment but has also led to her worrying about what other effects taking this wrongly given medication has had on her health. It has also caused her to feel more anxious during a time that has already been most challenging both mentally and physically.

We are both very upset given this serious mistake that has had a detrimental effect on my wife’s health and feel completely let down as well as having no trust in Fertility Plus. After informing them of their error and our concerns we were not even offered an apology. We sincerely hope no one else suffers as we have.

label.evidence_quote: given 2 months of the completely wrong medication

text:
It's taken a while for me to be able to write a review given what a terrible experience I had at Fertility Plus at the start of last year. After reading very positive, nearly perfect reviews about this fertility clinic, I'm honestly so disappointed at how out of touch and impersonal this fertility practice is.

I had some unfortunate news about my fertility results (very low AMH). I told Dr Thakre, that I had previously done blood tests for infertility and that my results had varied but she was concerned about a high FHS result that was previously documented. As a result, Dr Thakre said she wanted me to get another infertility test including an FSH test to confirm her consultation that I was in perimenopause. She told me that I could get the test done at the same lab that I had done my AMH test around the corner from the clinic after finishing our face to face appointment as she would be able to confirm her diagnosis the next day by phone call. Despite asking several questions at the appointment about the diagnosis, myself and my husband didn't leave feeling like we were any less informed about our journey ahead and the level of empathy provided came across as quite fake.

The next day Dr Thakre called and advised me that my FHS had skyrocketed and unfortunately as she had explained the day before that my only options for bearing children would be through a donor egg with 40-45% success or conceiving naturally (but the chances were very minimal). IVF with my own eggs was not an option. During our call I asked the doctor to provide information about the treatment options available and she had offered to book some therapy sessions for me before making a decision about a treatment plan to move ahead with donor eggs. She told me I should speak with my GP about hormone replacement therapy and to get my bone density checked due to perimenopause. She also reassured me I didn't have to make the decision right away about conceiving with a donor egg. We again asked a few questions regarding what might our journey ahead might be like, but we were again left feeling that our interaction was cold and transactional.

After the call I emailed to request a copy of my blood results. They emailed me back saying I had an outstanding bill of £234 and said they received my request and would get back to me shortly. After several back and forth questions I had regarding my blood tests, the reception team finally advised that they wanted me to pay for the additional blood test before they would release my own personal test information. Once I made the payment they released my results, and gave me the Gmail contact details of a therapist to pay for counselling sessions.

After both my in-person appointment, phone call and the correspondence since then, my gut instinct told me that something about the service we received wasn't right so I decided to get a second opinion. I'm thankful I trusted my gut and decided not to continue with this clinic and found somewhere that treated me less like a number and more like a human.
