text:
Overall, I feel deeply disappointed with my experience. Throughout the entire process, I was constantly assigned to different doctors. There was no continuity of care and no single person taking clear responsibility for my case. Each time I had to re-explain my situation, which was emotionally exhausting.

Communication has also been slow and inefficient. Responses often take too long, and it feels unclear who is actually overseeing or coordinating the treatment plan. In a process that is already physically and emotionally demanding, this lack of structure adds significant stress.

The overall efficiency of the clinic has been low, and the outcomes have not met expectations. It leaves me feeling confused and helpless about how things are managed internally.

I understand that medical treatment does not guarantee success. However, patients deserve consistency, accountability, and timely communication — especially in such a sensitive and important journey.

At this stage, I feel more tired than supported.

label.evidence_quote: no adequate shared decision-making

text:
My experience with King’s Fertility was very distressing, both clinically and administratively. On the clinical side, I did not feel there was adequate shared decision-making or clear explanation of options and rationale at key points, and parts of my treatment felt rushed. I left appointments unsure what had been decided and why, which is not acceptable in time-sensitive fertility care.

I felt pressured to proceed without a full discussion of alternatives and expected outcomes.
Medication/protocol decisions were communicated in a way that felt last-minute and not properly explained.
My concerns (including about timing/monitoring) weren’t taken seriously until I pushed repeatedly.

On the administrative side, communication was inconsistent and at times contradictory. I was told one plan by clinical staff and later told something different by reception/admin, including confusion around what was possible next and what pathway/funding applied. Monitoring and scheduling also felt poorly managed, and I repeatedly had to chase to get the right follow-up.

The worst moment was immediately after egg collection, while I was recovering from sedation and in pain, when booking/communication issues added unnecessary stress and felt lacking in compassion. Fertility treatment needs clarity, coordination, and empathy — unfortunately that wasn’t my experience here.

label.evidence_quote: issues that had been missed

text:
After two unsuccessful IVF cycles at King’s Fertility, one failed implantation and one early miscarriage, we decided to seek treatment elsewhere. Looking back, I truly regret the valuable time we lost before moving on.

At our new clinic, several issues that had been missed or not properly investigated at King’s were finally identified. Something as straightforward as checking anti-TPO antibodies had been overlooked, and once discovered, it was easily managed with levothyroxine. We were also concerned about my partner’s semen analysis. The sperm count of the sample that was used for egg fertilisation was suddenly 10 times higher than any of the past tests (including one just 2 weeks before). That was very concerning, nobody could explain it.

My stimulation cycle at King’s was difficult. I felt borderline overstimulated and, despite retrieving 21 oocytes, we ended up with only 4 embryos. At the time, I assumed this was normal. But at the new clinic, with 13 oocytes, we got 6 embryos. That difference made us realise things could have been handled more carefully.

What stood out most was the lack of transparency and explanation from the start. The whole process felt protocol-driven rather than personalised. Important tests and options, like sperm DNA fragmentation and PGT-A, were never mentioned until we researched them ourselves and asked elsewhere. I understand that doctors may doubt whether certain tests are medically necessary, but patients should still have the option to choose and pay for additional investigations if they might improve the chances of success.

Being an NHS-funded patient should not limit your ability to access or even learn about private options. Every patient deserves full information to make informed decisions.

A lot of people mention the lack of continuity of care at King’s. At the time, it didn’t seem like the biggest issue for us, but at our new clinic we saw the same doctor every time, and we now understand how valuable that continuity is. It shows that it is possible. The bigger issue at King’s was feeling like we had to research everything ourselves just to advocate for the right care.

I would have shared all of this directly with King’s, but they do not follow up with their patients. There was no chance to give feedback. This is especially noticeable because most clinics routinely send feedback requests and appointment reminders, which we never received from King’s. Allowing patients to share honest feedback is important if a clinic wants to improve and make sure future patients get the best possible experience.

Key points for anyone considering King’s Fertility
1.	Be proactive and educate yourself about your options, especially if you are an NHS patient.
2.	Advocate for yourself at every stage to make sure your treatment is personalised.
3.	Ask early about tests like sperm DNA fragmentation and PGT-A, not only after failures.
4.	Continuity of care matters, but transparency and proper investigation matter even more.

I hope this review helps other patients make informed decisions and encourages King’s Fertility to improve areas where it could really benefit future patients.

label.evidence_quote: uncertainty about fibroids

text:
Overall experience: negative

Positive experience with Dr Kuga and Dr Sadaf: clear communication, evidence-based discussion, and appropriate expectation-setting.
In contrast, the consultation with Mr Kuhan Rajah gave a 5–10% IVF success estimate despite uncertainty about whether fibroids were cavity-distorting or actually blocking ovarian access; this felt premature without proper mapping first (e.g., saline sonohysterography, MRI, or diagnostic hysteroscopy).
Options and approximate probabilities were not presented (such as “Option A ≈ X %” and “Option B ≈ Y %”) to let us make an informed financial decision; instead, the discussion felt discouraging, as if there was no chance, with limited exploration of alternatives and statistics.
We would like to  transfer care to another clinic. The records-release process was described as potentially taking up to a month with no guaranteed expedition, which—given time-sensitive decisions—left us feeling trapped at King’s.
A close family member who is a retired gynaecologist agrees that prognosis should follow complete diagnostics and encouraged a second opinion.
Not recommend.

text:
I think the response from King's Fertility (Jo Evans) to my Wife's negative review perfectly represents how insensitive, unprofessional and inappropriate this Fertility Clinic is towards their patients.

There have been countless failures which are far too long to list within our agonising 6 months period with this Clinic. All of which, King's Fertility have taken no accountability or ownership for and have provided NO apology. Instead, deflecting it back to us and coercively trying to take advantage of our vulnerabilities by stating we are the problem. Ever since we raised a formal complaint addressing their failures and incompetence, on several occasions they've attempted to convince us to leave the Clinic which is no way to treat a Patient who's raised a complaint. They should want to acknowledge their failures and do better! I'd also like to know what the "additional support and appointments are" these don't exist, it's a false claim.  Instead they brush over and ignore statements which they know are true with regards to their negligence and poor conduct. They seem to think doing this disproves our claims, but it doesn't.  There is absent communication, lack of organisation, constant careless mistakes, staff are mostly rude and condescending/patronising. We've wasted 60 hours of annual leave, petrol costs not mentioning the £2.5k they kept on credit until we requested a refund. They state we weren't charged for cancellation fees (a cancellation due to their mistakes), this was only due to my Wife and I making a formal complaint refusing to pay, evidencing their negligence, otherwise they'd happily of taken our money knowing full well they shouldn't be. Furthermore, the appointment mentioned on 29th October was missed by my Wife and I as we were, again, wrongfully informed over the phone by Admin that our appointment was booked for 30th October. This Clinic provides no appointment letters, E-Mails or texts, which I imagine is due to the many booking mistakes they make on a daily basis, which they don't want the patients to evidence. When we have asked for booking confirmation we are told to "make our own note", I wonder why!  I had actually called on 8th November 2024 but again due to the Clinics incompetence no notes were made on the system to reflect this, Admin said this was being looked into as the calls were recorded, I provided the date and the time and I assume the clinic was found to be in the wrong because I’ve heard nothing more on the situation. We've requested our medical records, as you're never verbally informed of scan reports or blood results, if you're lucky you'll get a "all is OK", "it's normal". The Clinic also won't provide summary reports, scans or blood results after each appointment, which is standard process in nearly every NHS Clinic. The fact we paid privately you'd think they'd afford us with a piece of paper. Upon requesting our medical records we were told this will take 28 days as allegedly they have had a high volume of requests! I imagine it's due to patients either moving clinic or the fact patients are wanting to know their results due to not being informed in their appointments.

King's Fertility does have a dispensing service, but it's outsourced! Another false fabrication as a poor attempt of the Clinic trying to save their reputation. King's Fertility charge a £50 dispensing fee and won't allow you to take the prescription yourself to source the medication at the best price! You must only obtain all medication through their designated dispenser, the items are extortionately priced. Most Clinics will write the prescription and allow the Patient to rightfully have control of where they wish to obtain it whether it's via the Clinic's recommended supplier or ASDA!

I find King's Fertility deeply insult our intelligence and are very disrespectful to even attempt to try point the blame to us when we can heavily evidence our valid cause to complain. 2 months later, our complaint remains not upheld. We will escalate our complaint to the appropriate authority.

Appalling experience at Kings. I've left some time before I complete this review, as I went elsewhere for treatment after Kings and had so much better results. Kings are not good for women like me with low amh, put me on highest dose of stims to fry my eggs, made me trigger when I had three 16 mm follicles (they were apparently worried about ovulation) which resulted in no mature eggs and obviously no fertilisation, lost £9k with no results! And there was no empathy afterwards.  Went to another London clinic to embryo batch and each cycle I've had few mature eggs and good quality blasts to freeze, with them knowing to trigger me at LEAST 18mm follicles. Traumatised by my experience at Kings and would not recommend them at all!
My follow up consultation after this failed expensive round was the most discouraging, giving me a 10% success rate and saying it is likely my egg quality.

I can say that moving clinics was the best decision in my life as I have now been blessed with twins, using my own eggs! I would not recommend Kings at all.

label.evidence_quote: Kings are not good for women like me with low amh

text:
I cannot stress enough how appalling my experience with King’s Fertility Clinic, London has been. I am beyond frustrated, and after filing a formal complaint that led to no changes, I am now considering seeking legal advice for potential misconduct.

In June 2024, I paid for an initial couple’s consultation with King’s Fertility Clinic, yet to this day, I have not received any actual fertility treatment. Instead, the clinic has endlessly pushed excessive diagnostic tests like bloodwork and scans, and even required me to get additional immunisations (boosters) and take iron supplements—both at extra costs. I've since learned these were unnecessary and had no relation to succeeding in a viable pregnancy. King's Fertility warned that they would refuse treatment if I didn’t comply with these recommendations (blackmailed).

King's Fertility Clinic has deliberately caused avoidable delays. For instance, not returning  my calls for weeks, set appointments with a 2-3 week wait, then claimed that my original blood tests were no longer valid under their policy requiring renewed blood tests every 4-6 weeks. This led to repeated blood tests at additional costs each time. Currently, King’s Fertility Clinic holds £2,500 of my money, yet none of this has gone towards the 3 cycles of IUI that I purchased.

When we finally attempted the first IUI cycle, I informed the consultant that I cannot ovulate without medication and need ovulation induction (e.g. Clomid). I provided detailed medical history from my previous NHS fertility treatments to confirm this. However, the consultant insisted on monitoring me to see if I could ovulate naturally. Throughout this first cycle, I had multiple follicle tracking scans, each showing immature follicles. Despite repeatedly asking/prompting about ovulation induction at each follicle tracking scan, I was dismissed by the nurse. At the last scan, the nurse informed me that my follicles were shrinking, and the IUI cycle would be cancelled. I was then charged a £500 cancellation fee. Solely due to King's Fertility incompetence, but I had no doubt in my mind, they did this deliberately to maximise profit.

Following a formal complaint, they switched my treatment plan to IVF. Yet, last month, King’s Fertility refused to begin treatment because I hadn’t had a period. They claimed I needed an “anytime” scan to determine the best time to start bleeding (an absurd statement) and insisted that I obtain progesterone tablets through them at an added cost. Despite having a prescription from my GP for Progesterone tablets to induce a bleed, they wouldn’t proceed with IVF since the medication was not issued by their clinic—another clear money-making tactic.

After I had a natural period, I contacted the clinic, only to be told by Admin that they need to have the consultant call me before starting IVF. That was two weeks ago, and I have yet to hear anything. It’s as though they’re frustrated that they couldn’t extract more money from me.

I wish I had taken previous reviews about this clinic more seriously. Five months of my life have been wasted, and I strongly urge others to avoid King’s Fertility Clinic. Their approach to exploiting vulnerable women desperate for a child to maximise their profits is utterly disgraceful. I'm absolutely furious and I'll be escalating my complaint.

label.evidence_quote: endlessly pushed excessive diagnostic tests

I regret having to express my dissatisfaction with Kings Fertility. As a private patient, the client-facing and communication aspects of their service are notably very poor.

Conflicting information from different receptionists regarding MDT meetings was frustrating, and challenging them resulted in them being insensitive, condescending and defensive. The telephone manner/etiquette of some of the receptionist is also very poor. The receptionists /admin staff will forever be the biggest downfall of the clinic - their attitude is very concerning and every interaction with them has been negative.

Furthermore, despite requests, no written correspondence or clinical letters from the assessments have been received, causing delays in necessary external referrals. The lack of continuity of care also makes it challenging to build rapport or trust with their doctors.

What is also concerning, is the absence of any formal ID checks.

This overall experience has been disappointing and has significantly impacted my confidence in their services.

text:
I was recommended to this clinic from a friend but I have been disappointed from the beginning. I initially contact them for fertility testing with a view to egg freezing and was excited that they could fit me in, were less expensive than other clinics, and were local to me. Unfortunately after I booked the appointment I received no confirmation email and was concerned the booking hadn't been made. I waited until after a long weekend to call them and when I spoke to someone she told me I'd been listed as an NHS patient instead of a private one and as such hadn't received the confirmation email. I asked if I could still have the appointment and she just kept saying "you have to pay" over and over again, as if I had deliberately tried not to pay. I explained that no one had ever billed me and I'd received no invoice. She confirmed I could keep the appointment and I then asked if she could move my follow up consultation earlier as they were making me wait for a week. She became strange at this point and said she had to check things with a manager and that someone would call me back.

6 hours later I received a phone call from them offering me the same consulation appointment. The woman also threatened that they would cancel the appointment if I didn't pay, again insinuating that I had deliberately tried not to pay. I told her I had called four times trying to confirm the appointment and at no point had I ever refused to pay. I then asked her to send me a confirmation email and paid almost £400 for the tests. She didn't send the confirmation email until the next day.

The scans themselves were fine and I had perfectly pleasant experience with the doctors and nurses there, but every time I tried to liaise with the admin it was awful. A week later I was supposed to have my follow up consultation and organised my work schedule around it. The doctor was then 15 minutes late calling me with no communication from anyone. I was just waiting by the phone at work. When the doctor finally called he didn't apologise for being late at all and gave me one of the most bland, yet functional consultations ever.

Of course I have been in search of a clinic to carry out the egg freezing process with and have had several consulations. I would have honestly loved to use Kings but it was impossible to trust them with the logistics of organising my procedure when my initial experience was so ridiculous. I'm sure the medical side is probably fine but I don't want to spend thousands to be mucked around by a clinic like this when there are so many others out there that treat you with a lot more respect for your time. Honestly really disappointed and didn't think this would be the case after my friend recommended them.

label.evidence_quote: received no confirmation email

I booked a private consultation costing £250 and experienced serious issues on two separate occasions.

The first appointment was delayed by around 30 minutes. I waited infront of my laptop but did not receive the Zoom link or any update. I had to send multiple follow-up emails and also tried calling, but received no answer. Only after I chased several times did they eventually contact me to apologise. This was a clear waste of my time and felt highly unprofessional for a paid private consultation. There was no real remediation for the inconvenience caused.

The second appointment was even worse. The consultation was delayed again — this time by approximately 45 minutes. I received no apology, no acknowledgement, and no communication at the time of the appointment despite following up twice by email to ask when it would start. In the end, no consultation was attended at all. I requested a full refund, and only then did I receive a response.

It is unacceptable for a private clinic charging £250 per consultation to manage appointments in this way and to show such disregard for patients’ time. Appointment scheduling and time management are the responsibility of the clinic, and patients should not be left waiting without updates or clear communication.

I also found it very unclear why the clinic insisted on online consultations when I repeatedly expressed a preference for a face-to-face appointment. The communication around this felt vague, and even basic details such as the clinic setup and process were not clearly explained, which added to the overall sense of disorganisation.

Overall, I found the experience disorganised, unprofessional, and stressful. I would not recommend this clinic based on how my appointments were handled.

Some excellent staff, but systemic failures in care and ethics:
We had a polarised experience here. While some of the team (Maartje in nursing, Ruth in admin) and specific doctors like Dr. Amanda and Dr. Anna are fantastic, the clinic’s management and diagnostic approach were deeply disappointing and frankly frightening.

The positives: If you are assigned the team mentioned above, you are in good hands.

The concerns (shortlist out of many):

Lack of holistic investigation: Despite being 35+ and raising concerns on initial test results, our requests for further testing were dismissed. Its all put on the women when men are 50% of the equation. We felt pushed into expensive, repetitive cycles without investigating the unexplored causes. Don't rush, push to do thorough investigative work (not just surface level tests especially for both partners) before you start any rounds.

Financial priority over care: After an unexpected failed round where we had clear fertilisation issues, the doctor refused to answer clarifying questions via email, insisting on a £250 appointment fee. At such a vulnerable time, this felt exploitative. Our experience at other clinics is entirely different where Doctors' answer our questions and work with us not just talk at us.

Administrative negligence: Most distressingly, our embryos were moved even after we had explicitly withdrawn consent.

We spent upwards of £30k and felt like a transaction rather than patients. We have since moved to a clinic that prioritises investigation over rushing rounds. Please do your research before committing and avoid at all costs if you are above 35 and don't have either of those doctors.

text: I do not recommend Dr. Bataille or The Medical Chambers Kensington. The skin check was shockingly superficial, and the interaction dismissive. The billing was confusing and non-transparent, with unexpected double charges (one for the doctor, one for the clinic - both, overpriced). When I raised concerns, the response was slow, dismissive and disrespectful towards the patient, including repeatedly mischaracterising reasons for concern. This isn’t about my diagnosis or outcome—it’s about respecting patients’ rights to clear, honest information and involvement in their care. Unacceptable.

text:
December 2024. Be very careful: after being a client for 10 years, I learnt last week that some invoices, dated from NINE months ago, hadn't been covered by my insurance company (not sure why, but maybe). Medical Chambers just decided to charge my card, without giving me even a month delay (when I waited 9 months to know that there was an issue). This is just a business, definitely not "client centric" as they claim to be.
UPDATE Feb 2025: they now claim that the mistake is mine because they found an email dated 2023 (!!) in which I made a mistake regarding my insurance company. But this email was for one specific appointment, for which they managed to process my insurance details without any issues :-)
And I double check my insurance details every single time I walk in, with their reception staff ... anyway, they obviously won't take responsibility for anything: you, as clients, will always be in the wrong.

label.evidence_quote: Medical Chambers just decided to charge

text:
I have visited this place in order to see a qualified GP. I was more than happy to go private as I hoped I would save time and get better more personalised care.
In reality this experience was quite awful.
As their GP diagnosed that I don't have an ear infection (the problem that made me want to see a GP in the first place), but instead I may have a very serious neurological disorder and sent me to an emergency room that same evening.
After waiting for hours in an emergency section of the hospital and experiencing a lot of anxiety about the possibility that she presented me with I saw the ENT specialist. As soon as the specialist looked into my ear she confirmed that I have an ear infection and should be treated with antibiotics.
Why did I go private? Spend money? Get misdiagnosed, scared and still end up in a public hospital where I got much better care?
My advice.. AVOID THIS PLACE

label.evidence_quote: In reality this experience was quite awful

text:
If you are not even honest about the small things, how can you expect others to believe you will be honest about the big things? Let me tell you about my experience:

They have a policy where they charge you £2 for a mask, which at the beginning of the crisis at least they did *not* notify patients (or, at least, me) about when booking the appointment over the phone (maybe it was in their fine print)

I said at reception that I did not want to pay the fee. They offered to give me a mask for free, so I went ahead with the appointment. However, they went ahead and charged my card. I sent them an email which did not get replied to for three months. I received a reply saying that their sign outside the clinic clearly states that masks cost £2. I wrote back to the person re-explaining that I did not ask to purchase a mask but was offered one for free. However, she did not reply for a month. I called today and a lady with an uncooperative attitude picked up the phone, first saying to me her manager was on another line, then saying she was not willing to speak to me and asking me to send another email. I visited MEDii Health in London and was disappointed with the level of professionalism during my appointment.

Additionally, I was not informed in advance that paying with a Chinese bank card would incur an extra handling fee. This fee was only revealed at the point of payment, and no detailed receipt or itemised invoice was provided. Clear and fair communication about fees is essential in private healthcare.

Overall, I expected a more transparent and professional experience from a private clinic.

text:
When my mother suggested she wanted cosmetic surgery including derma filler to regain her youthful appearance, I was concerned. Having worked in the pharmaceutical industry I was aware of problems relating to the use of derma fillers on the complexion of someone in their advancing years.

She had searched online and viewed a YouTube video of Dr Ayham Al-Ayoubi at London Medical and Aesthetic Clinic who we understood had worked with some high profile faces so must be reputable. Upon consultation he said it would be no problem and although we weren't expecting it to be a cut and dry process, we also weren't expecting my mother to have such a disastrous reaction to the derma filler Sculptra.

Large swellings on her cheeks - expected to distend  - remained prominent after several weeks and began affecting her state of mind. When we questioned why this product was used on someone with age-related fat loss and relatively thin skin, we were told that it wouldn't be a long term problem as it would reduce over time and that further treatments were recommended to encounter the irregularity.

These turned out to be just as damaging and the results were worse. This led us to believe that either Dr Al-Ayoubi was overstating his skill as a plastic technician - as this product was clearly unsuitable - or we were being taken for a ride financially. Either way the results were looking irreversible. Questioning his integrity proved futile as he wasn't about to admit liability, so here we are three years down the road and no solution or redress.

label.evidence_quote: concerned about derma fillers

text:
TOTAL SCAMMERS!  On 16 February, I booked an appointment with dermatologist Dr. Geoffrey Brent for Friday the 20th. However, immediately after completing the booking, I received a phone call from the clinic informing me that the doctor was not available on the 20th and that the earliest available appointment was actually on the 23rd.
It appears that the doctor’s schedule was not up to date in the clinic’s booking system. When I made the reservation, several time slots were shown as available for the 20th. However, during the phone call, I was informed that there was no availability at all on that day. As a result, the clinic cancelled my appointment. And shortly after the call , I received an email confirming the cancellation. So basically ,booking appointment, receiving call from the clinic to cancel, then receiving the cancellation email ; they all happened in less than an hour.
The following day, I noticed that I had nevertheless been charged for the cancelled appointment. Since then, I have been trying to get a refund, but for some reason the clinic’s accounts/credit department has not processed it.
They are withholding people’s money without any valid reason!!! It’s been a week I am still trying to get my £275!!!
AVOID FROM THIS CLINIC! I will be warning everyone to avoid this clinic at all costs!
If my refund is not processed within 48 hours (the legal deadline), I will begin formal action to recover my money. As a first step, I will file an official complaint through ADR. I will also report the matter to Trading Standards. If necessary, I am also fully prepared to pursue the claim through the SCC as well.

label.evidence_quote: doctor’s schedule was not up to date

text:
I had a very disappointing experience with LIPS Healthcare due to poor communication and lack of clarity throughout the process.

When I first called, I was told I could receive the service and have a doctor consultation afterwards without needing to book an appointment. To be safe, I called again on the day to double check, but the staff sounded unsure and quickly booked me an appointment instead, which already caused confusion.

When I arrived at the clinic, there were very few instructions and no clear explanation of what to expect. I felt confused and anxious the entire time. I was then informed that there was no doctor available on site, which completely contradicted what I had been told previously.

Although I tried to remain calm and trust their professionalism, the information provided was unclear and inconsistent. When I later emailed to ask for clarification, I was told that this explanation was not included and that I would need to pay for an additional service just to have my results explained.

Overall, the experience felt unprofessional, poorly managed, and extremely stressful. I would not recommend this clinic to anyone who values clear communication and reliable healthcare guidance.

label.evidence_quote: poor communication and lack of clarity

text:

text:
Recently I had a negative experience with neurologist Dr. Gratwicke. I was prescribed an expensive (£600), strong migraine medication despite having long-standing neurological hypersensitivity (my nervous system can’t tolerate even caffeine contents of a single cup of tea) and symptoms that did not clearly fit a migraine pattern. I specifically asked about the certainty of the diagnosis and was assured it was correct.

After taking the medication, I experienced severe adverse effects — chest heaviness, breathing difficulties, throat constriction, and acute neurological tension — requiring an A&E visit and leaving me unable to work for a week, with lingering effects for weeks afterward.

It then took 25 days to receive a response from the consultant. The reply stated that my symptoms were “quite unusual” and “might not be related,” even though shortness of breath is listed as a known side effect of the medication occurring in 1 in 100 people that requires immediate medical attention.

My concerns relate to clinical judgment, a mismatch between my symptoms and the diagnosis, slow communication, and a lack of recognition of the seriousness of what happened.

Clinic never responded to my subsequent customer experience email.

Update after customer complaints response: they looked into it, found no breach of prescribing policy and never responded to my email asking what steps they'll take to implement changes that would: 1) improve communication around diagnostic uncertainty, 2) ensure patients with known hypersensitivity have risks discussed in a transparent, thorough, and reassuring manner and 3) ensure timely follow-ups when patients report significant side effects.

label.evidence_quote: severe adverse effects

As I booked few days ago I didn’t remember I already made the payment online at the time of booking. They took payment at reception before the appointment. text:
DO NOT USE THIS SERVICE!

I then have to chase multiple times, emailing and calling them, and 2 weeks later they say: we have no information about a knee brace being ordered, we'll give you a call by the end of this week to clarify what's going on.

So that's 3 weeks just to receive a phone call! I've had a torn PCL which has meant I cannot go into work. I was told it needed to be put in a brace as soon as possible. I tell them this and they say, 'we'll give you a call by the end of this week'. It's Tuesday today... It's outrageous.

I can't believe how poorly this has been handled and the lack of urgency from their side (which could have massive implications on people's long term health) is staggering.

Stay away and find a clinic that takes your health seriously.

text:
I came to buy medicine at this pharmacy when my GP told me it was the same price to buy it as it was prescribed. This pharmacy was selling the medicine at over £30 - when I questioned this, they informed me it was this price everywhere and I wasn't going to get a better deal. I trusted them and bought it. I later discovered that at EVERY pharmacy in London it is between £11-14.
Furious at this I left a two star review, rating the pharmacy as overpriced.
The manager of this pharmacy searched my name from the review and proceeded TO COME TO MY WORK PLACE to tell me to take it down. This was deeply uncomfortable and unprofessional - and caused friction with my management. They pressured me into taking down the post and I was left feeling embarrassed and harassed.
I ended up reporting the pharmacy to the board of Pharmacies in the UK. They informed me the behaviour was unacceptable but there was nothing they could do.
The whole experience was shocking.
I'm only putting my review back up because I have moved jobs to one no longer within walking distance. Do not go here.

label.evidence_quote: selling the medicine at over £30

text: I’d like to first of all say that my review does not reflect the space itself which is very clean or the people who were mostly respectful and nice BUT I have to stand up for myself as a paying individual against misinformation and misleading people when it comes to health costs. I went to LIPS for an ENT appointment. At the time of the appointment, Dr. Wong offered to perform a small procedure (I believe it was a nasal endoscopy but I’m not sure) which took about 5-10 mins. When I inquired as to the cost (as one does because private medical is very costly), he said somewhere in the £150-200 range and I said OK. At the end of the appointment, we settled the price at £495 at the Lips front desk, the initial consultation fee along with the cost of the additional procedure and I paid this the day of the appointment in July 2025 which I confirmed with the Lips team. Feb 2026 (7 MONTHS LATER) I am receiving notice of another £510 facility fee. I expressed my confusion to both Lips and Dr. Wongs team as this was never disclosed to me as an additional cost at the time of the appointment and I am only now alerted to this cost in writing 7 months after my appointment. Had I been notified of a facility fee in addition to the procedure cost I already paid of £495, I would have never agreed to the procedure or even the appointment. I feel very mislead and upset with the whole situation and will say not be returning to LIPS again as I feel they were not cost transparent at all which is so necessary when so many medical facilities can be so predatory towards scared individuals seeking help and assurance.

label.evidence_quote: misleading people when it comes to health costs

text:
UPDATE: On return I had booked my 2 day PCR test that was supposed to arrive on 13th December. It did not arrive, I am once again on the phone with customer services who are struggling to understand what has happened (again) and have no PCR test...

DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY

Today I upgraded to an express 4-hour test. I took this test at 9am GMT with results expected to be sent by 1pm GMT latest.

At 1pm I did not receive my results. I tried to call but there was a very long wait (I was number 79 in the line), so I went back to the local clinic where I had the test in the morning where the manager and her colleague tried their best to help me.

They had to call several people and I waited in the clinic over an hour and a half to get to the bottom of what had happened. Simply, my test had not been processed and if I had not gone into the clinic, I have no idea if it would have been. At this point people in my business were waiting for that information with a knock-on impact. Not only that but I had to take 3 hours out of work to deal with the issue and my results were over 2 hours late.

I understand there was one other gentleman waiting to get his express test with the same issue, only he was waiting to visit his sick child in hospital and could not do so until the test was returned. I can only imagine how frustrating it was for him.

I have emailed and asked for an explanation for the inexcusable experience I have had with this company as well as a full refund. I did not expect to pay almost £400 for something they could not deliver, this is misleading.

All this being said, the staff in the Kingston branch could not have been better and I would like to thank them for helping however they could .

label.evidence_quote: did not receive results

text:
Everyone there are amazingly friendly, and I really felt looked after.

However, as someone who has stage 4 and 5 osteoarthritis in both hips, I feel that lipogems procedure probably shouldn't have been offered.

There seemed to be some confusion around recovery times. At consultation I was told 3 months, a former patient who had success, told me 1-2 weeks, on the day I was told a maximum of 6 months?

Doing everything to support my healing, with physio and a low inflammation diet, did cause some easing, however Im unsure if that's the procedures influence.

9months later, I'm back to looking at replacements, due to an increase of pain and lack of mobility. In addition to this the xrays (at my expense) after the procedure showed no improvement.

Sadly it would seem I must fall into the 20% that don't have any success.

If you have a year to waste and private insurance to foot the bill, then I'd say go for it. However if you're self funding and have quite advanced stages, then I'd say this probably isn't for you.

And be mindful that it will be registered as a pre existing condition/treatment on your records, which may implicate further treatments, depending on duration and who you're with.

Wanted to share some hindsights honestly so that it maybe become someone's foresight.

Best wishes

K

label.evidence_quote: confusion around recovery times

The person who gave me the kit said, "you have to do this test when you get back to the UK" and that was all. I left the test at home so I didn't have to carry it with me in the USA, but took a photo of the box which had a barcode with a 9 digit alphanumeric identifier under it - as I did with all of my other Covid test docs, travel insurance, etc. The night before my return flight when I checked in online, there was a notification from the airline that I had to fill in an online passenger locator form which asked for the day 2 'reference number'. When I entered the number on the box it was rejected and I couldn't complete the form. I couldn't find out why, and the next morning I emailed the Regenerative Clinic but didn't get a reply, then managed to call my wife who found the test kit, read the instructions, paid the fee of £39, and I managed to fill in the form. I wish it had been made clear that all of this had to be done before I returned to the UK. Who needs added stress at a time when travel is incredibly stressful anyway? It took hours for me to sort this out. I went to the Brighton clinic and spoke to a member of staff who apologised for the mix up, but she agreed that the process involving arrivals tests is not very clear. So why wasn't that point made when the test kit was given to me? Altogether I paid £118 for tests, plus additional phone and data costs incurred in the US but words are free to dispense - I would like to have heard more information and instruction when the test was first given to me.

label.evidence_quote: all went well
